papers.title: Physics-Guided Dimension Reduction for Simulation-Free Operator Learning of Stiff Differential--Algebraic Systems
papers.summary: Neural surrogates for stiff differential-algebraic equations (DAEs) face two key challenges: soft-constraint methods leave algebraic residuals that stiffness amplifies into large errors, while hard-constraint methods require trajectory data from computationally expensive stiff integrators. We introduce an extended Newton implicit layer that enforces algebraic consistency and quasi-steady-state reduction within a single differentiable solve. Given slow-state predictions from a physics-informed DeepONet, the proposed layer recovers fast and algebraic states, eliminates the stiffness-amplification pathway within each time window, and reduces the output dimension to the slow states alone. Gradients derived via the implicit function theorem capture a stiffness-scaled coupling term that is absent in penalty-based approaches. Cascaded implicit layers further extend the framework to multi-component systems with provable convergence. On a grid-forming inverter DAE (21 states), the proposed method (7 outputs, 1.42 percent error) significantly outperforms penalty methods (39.3 percent), standard Newton approaches (57.0 percent), and augmented Lagrangian or feedback linearization baselines, which fail to converge. Two independently trained models compose into a 44-state system without retraining, achieving 0.72 to 1.16 percent error with zero algebraic residual. Conformal prediction further provides 90 percent coverage in-distribution and enables automatic out-of-distribution detection.

ai.tldr: 提出一种扩展牛顿隐式层，结合物理信息DeepONet，实现刚性微分代数系统的无仿真算子学习，显著降低输出维度并消除刚度放大误差。
ai.motivation: 现有神经代理方法处理刚性DAE时，软约束方法因刚度放大代数残差导致大误差，硬约束方法依赖昂贵积分器生成轨迹数据。
ai.method: 设计扩展牛顿隐式层，在单次可微求解中强制执行代数一致性和准稳态约简；结合物理信息DeepONet预测慢状态，隐式层恢复快状态和代数状态；通过隐函数定理导出刚度缩放耦合梯度。
ai.result: 在21状态电网逆变器DAE上，所提方法（7输出，1.42%误差）显著优于惩罚方法（39.3%）和标准牛顿方法（57.0%）；两个独立模型组合成44状态系统无需重训练，误差0.72-1.16%，代数残差为零；共形预测实现90%分布内覆盖和自动分布外检测。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效、精确的刚性DAE神经代理方法，适用于复杂多组件系统建模，减少对昂贵仿真数据的依赖。

idea_spark.idea: 将扩展牛顿隐式层与图神经网络结合，处理具有拓扑结构的DAE系统。
idea_spark.risk: 隐式层求解可能引入额外计算开销，且对非光滑系统收敛性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用隐函数定理捕捉刚度缩放耦合项，为设计更鲁棒的物理信息神经网络提供新思路。

en.tldr: Proposes an extended Newton implicit layer combined with physics-informed DeepONet for simulation-free operator learning of stiff differential-algebraic systems, significantly reducing output dimension and eliminating stiffness-amplified errors.
en.motivation: Existing neural surrogates for stiff DAEs suffer from either large errors due to stiffness-amplified algebraic residuals (soft constraints) or reliance on expensive stiff integrators for trajectory data (hard constraints).
en.method: Introduces an extended Newton implicit layer that enforces algebraic consistency and quasi-steady-state reduction within a single differentiable solve; uses a physics-informed DeepONet to predict slow states, and the implicit layer recovers fast and algebraic states; gradients derived via the implicit function theorem capture a stiffness-scaled coupling term.
en.result: On a 21-state grid-forming inverter DAE, the proposed method (7 outputs, 1.42% error) significantly outperforms penalty methods (39.3%) and standard Newton approaches (57.0%); two independently trained models compose into a 44-state system without retraining, achieving 0.72-1.16% error with zero algebraic residual; conformal prediction provides 90% in-distribution coverage and automatic out-of-distribution detection.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient and accurate neural surrogate method for stiff DAEs, suitable for modeling complex multi-component systems while reducing reliance on expensive simulation data.

idea_spark.idea: Combine the extended Newton implicit layer with graph neural networks to handle DAE systems with topological structure.
idea_spark.risk: The implicit layer solve may introduce additional computational overhead, and convergence for non-smooth systems requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging the implicit function theorem to capture stiffness-scaled coupling terms offers new insights for designing more robust physics-informed neural networks.

papers.title: Fourier Weak SINDy: Spectral Test Function Selection for Robust Model Identification
papers.summary: We introduce Fourier Weak SINDy, a minimal noise-robust and interpretable derivative-free equation learning method that combines weak-form sparse equation learning with spectral density estimation for data-driven test function selection. By using orthogonal sinusoidal test functions inspired by their prevalence in Modulating Function-based system identification, the weak-form sparse regression problem reduces to a regression over Fourier coefficients. Dominant frequencies are then selected via multitaper estimation of the frequency spectrum of the data. This formulation unifies weak-form learning and spectral estimation within a compact and flexible framework. We illustrate the effectiveness of this approach in numerical experiments across multiple chaotic and hyperchaotic ODE benchmarks.

ai.tldr: 提出Fourier Weak SINDy方法，结合弱形式稀疏学习和谱密度估计，实现无导数、抗噪声的方程识别。
ai.motivation: 现有弱形式方法依赖人工选择测试函数，缺乏鲁棒性和自动化。
ai.method: 采用正交正弦测试函数将回归转化为傅里叶系数回归，并通过多窗谱估计选择主导频率。
ai.result: 在多个混沌和超混沌ODE基准上验证了方法的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种自动选择测试函数、抗噪声的方程学习方法，适用于含噪数据。

idea_spark.idea: 将谱估计与稀疏回归统一，实现测试函数自适应选择。
idea_spark.risk: 对非平稳或极短时间序列可能频率分辨率不足。
idea_spark.inspiration: 调制函数法中正弦函数的成功启发将弱形式与傅里叶分析结合。

zh.tldr: 提出Fourier Weak SINDy方法，结合弱形式稀疏学习和谱密度估计，实现无导数、抗噪声的方程识别。

en.tldr: Fourier Weak SINDy combines weak-form sparse learning with spectral density estimation for derivative-free, noise-robust equation discovery.
en.motivation: Existing weak-form methods rely on manually chosen test functions, lacking robustness and automation.
en.method: Uses orthogonal sinusoidal test functions to reduce regression to Fourier coefficients, with dominant frequencies selected via multitaper spectral estimation.
en.result: Demonstrated effectiveness on multiple chaotic and hyperchaotic ODE benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Provides an automated, noise-robust equation learning approach with adaptive test function selection.

idea_spark.idea: Unify spectral estimation and sparse regression for adaptive test function selection.
idea_spark.risk: May suffer from insufficient frequency resolution for non-stationary or very short time series.
idea_spark.inspiration: Success of sinusoidal functions in modulating function methods inspired the Fourier-weak integration.

papers.title: Tokenised Flow Matching for Hierarchical Simulation Based Inference
papers.summary: The cost of simulator evaluations is a key practical bottleneck for Simulation Based Inference (SBI). In hierarchical settings with shared global parameters and exchangeable site-level parameters and observations, this structure can be exploited to improve simulation efficiency. Existing hierarchical SBI approaches factorise the posterior yet still simulate across multiple sites per training sample; We instead explore likelihood factorisation (LF) to train from single-site simulations. In LF sampling we learn a per-site neural surrogate of the simulator and then assemble synthetic multi-site observations to amortise inference for the full hierarchical posterior. Building on this, we propose Tokenised Flow Matching for Posterior Estimation (TFMPE), a tokenised flow matching approach that supports function-valued observations through likelihood factorisation. To enable systematic evaluation, we introduce a benchmark for hierarchical SBI. We validate TFMPE on this benchmark and on realistic infectious disease and computational fluid dynamics models, finding well-calibrated posteriors while reducing computational cost.

ai.tldr: 提出Tokenised Flow Matching for Posterior Estimation (TFMPE)，通过似然分解和单站点模拟训练神经替代模型，实现层次化模拟推断的高效后验估计。
ai.motivation: 模拟评估成本是层次化模拟推断的主要瓶颈，现有方法仍需多站点联合模拟，效率不足。
ai.method: 利用似然分解从单站点模拟训练神经替代模型，再组装合成多站点观测，采用tokenised流匹配支持函数值观测的后验估计。
ai.result: 在基准测试、传染病模型和计算流体动力学模型上验证，后验校准良好且计算成本显著降低。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过似然分解和流匹配技术，在层次化模拟推断中大幅减少模拟开销。

idea_spark.idea: 将tokenised流匹配与函数值观测结合，为复杂模拟器提供灵活的替代模型。
idea_spark.risk: 神经替代模型可能在高维或非线性模拟中引入近似误差，需谨慎校准。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴自然语言处理中的tokenisation思想，将连续观测离散化为token序列，便于流匹配处理。

en.tldr: Proposes Tokenised Flow Matching for Posterior Estimation (TFMPE), which trains neural surrogates on single-site simulations via likelihood factorisation to efficiently estimate hierarchical posteriors.
en.motivation: Simulator evaluation cost is a key bottleneck in hierarchical SBI; existing methods still require multi-site joint simulations, limiting efficiency.
en.method: Exploits likelihood factorisation to train per-site neural surrogates from single-site simulations, then assembles synthetic multi-site observations; uses tokenised flow matching to handle function-valued observations.
en.result: Validated on a benchmark, infectious disease model, and computational fluid dynamics model, achieving well-calibrated posteriors with significantly reduced computational cost.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how likelihood factorisation and flow matching can drastically reduce simulation overhead in hierarchical SBI.

idea_spark.idea: Combining tokenised flow matching with function-valued observations provides a flexible surrogate for complex simulators.
idea_spark.risk: Neural surrogates may introduce approximation errors in high-dimensional or highly nonlinear simulators, requiring careful calibration.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from tokenisation in NLP, discretising continuous observations into token sequences for flow matching.

papers.title: Predictivity and Utility of Neural Surrogates of Multiscale PDEs
papers.summary: Scientific machine learning is increasingly being spoken of as universal emulators for classical numerical solvers for multi-scale partial differential equations, but most apparent successes can be explained by facts that also define their limits. Many successful benchmarks live on low-dimensional solution manifolds where any competent reduced model will interpolate well. More fundamentally, neural surrogates systematically under-resolve high-frequency content due to spectral bias, and coarse-graining compounds this problem through irreversible information loss. In many multi-scale problems, no architecture or training procedure can fully recover what the coarse representation discards. Two simple examples are used to characterize spectral bias, coarse-graining and error accumulation. We discuss why medium-range weather prediction on reanalysis data sits in a favorable sweet spot and why this will not generalize to genuinely chaotic multi-scale scenarios. We identify domains where neural surrogates offer genuine value, propose further research on neural-classical hybrids, and call for better reporting standards.

ai.tldr: 本文指出神经替代模型在多尺度偏微分方程中因谱偏差和粗粒化导致高频信息丢失，仅在低维解流形或中等范围天气预报等有利场景有效，并呼吁发展神经-经典混合方法。
ai.motivation: 揭示神经替代模型在多尺度PDE模拟中的根本局限，避免过度乐观的宣称。
ai.method: 通过两个简单例子刻画谱偏差、粗粒化和误差累积，并分析天气预报等案例。
ai.result: 神经替代模型在低维流形和中等范围天气预报中表现良好，但在真正混沌多尺度场景中无法泛化。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究者理解神经替代模型的适用边界，避免在不适用的多尺度问题上浪费资源。

idea_spark.idea: 设计神经-经典混合求解器，利用经典方法处理高频成分，神经网络负责低频或粗尺度部分。
idea_spark.risk: 混合方法可能增加实现复杂度，且接口处的信息传递仍需谨慎处理。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴多尺度建模中粗粒化与细尺度校正的思想，将神经网络作为粗尺度校正器而非完全替代者。

en.tldr: This paper argues that neural surrogates for multiscale PDEs suffer from spectral bias and irreversible information loss due to coarse-graining, performing well only on low-dimensional manifolds or medium-range weather prediction, and calls for neural-classical hybrids.
en.motivation: To reveal fundamental limitations of neural surrogates in multiscale PDE simulation and temper over-optimistic claims.
en.method: Characterizes spectral bias, coarse-graining, and error accumulation via two simple examples, and analyzes cases like weather prediction.
en.result: Neural surrogates work well on low-dimensional manifolds and medium-range weather prediction but fail to generalize to genuinely chaotic multiscale scenarios.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers understand the applicability boundaries of neural surrogates, avoiding wasted effort on unsuitable multiscale problems.

idea_spark.idea: Design neural-classical hybrid solvers where classical methods handle high-frequency components and neural networks handle low-frequency or coarse scales.
idea_spark.risk: Hybrid approaches may increase implementation complexity and require careful handling of information transfer at interfaces.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw inspiration from multiscale modeling where coarse-graining is combined with fine-scale corrections, using neural networks as coarse-scale correctors rather than full replacements.

papers.title: scpFormer: A Foundation Model for Unified Representation and Integration of the Single-Cell Proteomics
papers.summary: The integration of single-cell proteomic data is often hindered by the fragmented nature of targeted antibody panels. To address this limitation, we introduce scpFormer, a transformer-based foundation model designed for single-cell proteomics. Pre-trained on over 390 million cells, scpFormer replaces standard index-based tokenization with a continuous, sequence-anchored approach. By combining Evolutionary Scale Modeling (ESM) with value-aware expression embeddings, it dynamically maps variable panels into a shared semantic space without artificial discretization. We demonstrate that scpFormer generates global cell representations that perform competitively in large-scale batch integration and unsupervised clustering. Moreover, its open-vocabulary architecture facilitates in silico panel expansion, assisting in the reconstruction of biological manifolds in sparse clinical datasets. Finally, this learned protein co-expression logic is transferable to bulk-omics tasks, supporting applications like cancer drug response prediction. scpFormer provides a versatile, panel-agnostic framework to facilitate scalable biomarker discovery and precision oncology.

ai.tldr: 提出scpFormer，一个基于Transformer的单细胞蛋白质组学基础模型，通过连续序列锚定和ESM嵌入统一不同抗体面板的数据。
ai.motivation: 单细胞蛋白质组学数据因靶向抗体面板碎片化而难以整合，现有方法依赖离散化索引，无法灵活处理可变面板。
ai.method: 采用连续序列锚定替代标准索引分词，结合进化尺度建模（ESM）与值感知表达嵌入，将可变面板动态映射到共享语义空间。
ai.result: 在大规模批次整合和无监督聚类中表现优异；支持虚拟面板扩展，重建稀疏临床数据的生物流形；蛋白质共表达逻辑可迁移至批量组学任务。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户统一分析不同抗体面板的单细胞蛋白质组数据，实现面板无关的生物标志物发现和精准肿瘤学应用。

idea_spark.idea: 将scpFormer的连续嵌入方法应用于其他组学数据（如单细胞转录组或表观组）的跨面板整合。
idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖大规模预训练数据，在罕见疾病或小样本场景下泛化能力可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受自然语言处理中开放词汇模型启发，将蛋白质视为连续语义单元而非离散token。

idea_spark.idea: 将scpFormer的连续嵌入方法应用于其他组学数据（如单细胞转录组或表观组）的跨面板整合。
idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖大规模预训练数据，在罕见疾病或小样本场景下泛化能力可能受限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受自然语言处理中开放词汇模型启发，将蛋白质视为连续语义单元而非离散token。

en.tldr: scpFormer is a transformer-based foundation model for single-cell proteomics that unifies diverse antibody panels via continuous sequence-anchored tokenization and ESM embeddings.
en.motivation: Single-cell proteomic data integration is hindered by fragmented targeted antibody panels; existing methods rely on discrete indexing and cannot flexibly handle variable panels.
en.method: Replaces standard index-based tokenization with continuous sequence-anchored approach, combining Evolutionary Scale Modeling (ESM) with value-aware expression embeddings to map variable panels into a shared semantic space.
en.result: Achieves competitive performance in large-scale batch integration and unsupervised clustering; enables in silico panel expansion and reconstruction of biological manifolds in sparse clinical data; learned protein co-expression logic transfers to bulk-omics tasks like cancer drug response prediction.
en.help_to_user: Helps users integrate single-cell proteomic data from different antibody panels, enabling panel-agnostic biomarker discovery and precision oncology applications.

idea_spark.idea: Apply scpFormer's continuous embedding approach to cross-panel integration of other omics data (e.g., single-cell transcriptomics or epigenomics).
idea_spark.risk: Model relies on large-scale pretraining data; generalization may be limited in rare diseases or small-sample scenarios.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by open-vocabulary models in NLP, treating proteins as continuous semantic units rather than discrete tokens.

papers.title: A Multi-Plant Machine Learning Framework for Emission Prediction, Forecasting, and Control in Cement Manufacturing
papers.summary: Cement production is among the largest contributors to industrial air pollution, emitting ~3 Mt NOx/year. The industry-standard mitigation approach, selective non-catalytic reduction (SNCR), exhibits low NH3 utilization efficiency, resulting in operational inefficiencies and increased reagent costs. Here, we develop a data-driven framework for emission control using large-scale operational data from four cement plants worldwide. Benchmarking nine machine learning architectures, we observe that prediction error varies ~3-5x across plants due to variation in data richness. Incorporating short-term process history nearly triples NOx prediction accuracy, revealing that NOx formation carries substantial process memory, a timescale dependence that is absent in CO and CO2. Further, we develop models that forecast NOx overshoots as early as nine minutes, providing a buffer for operational adjustments. The developed framework controls NOx formation at the source, reducing NH3 consumption in downstream SNCR. Surrogate model projections estimate a ~34-64% reduction in NOx while preserving clinker quality, corresponding to a reduction of ~290 t NOx/year and ~58,000 USD/year in NH3 savings. This work establishes a generalizable framework for data-driven emission control, offering a pathway toward low-emission operation without structural modifications or additional hardware, with potential applicability to other hard-to-abate industries such as steel, glass, and lime.

ai.tldr: 基于四家水泥厂的大规模运行数据，开发了一个数据驱动的多工厂机器学习框架，用于预测、预报和控制NOx排放，并评估了多种模型架构。
ai.motivation: 水泥生产是工业空气污染的主要来源，现有SNCR脱硝技术氨利用率低、成本高，亟需高效、低成本的排放控制方法。
ai.method: 收集四家水泥厂运行数据，对比九种机器学习架构，引入短期过程历史特征，构建NOx超限提前9分钟预报模型，并开发替代模型优化SNCR操作。
ai.result: 短期历史数据使NOx预测精度提升近3倍；替代模型预计可减少NOx排放34-64%，每年节省约58,000美元氨消耗。
ai.help_to_user: 该框架无需硬件改造即可实现排放控制，可推广至钢铁、玻璃等其他难减排行业。

idea_spark.risk: 模型依赖数据质量与工厂运行条件差异，跨厂泛化需进一步验证。

en.tldr: A data-driven multi-plant machine learning framework was developed using operational data from four cement plants to predict, forecast, and control NOx emissions, benchmarking nine architectures.
en.motivation: Cement production is a major industrial air polluter; current SNCR technology suffers from low NH3 utilization and high cost, necessitating efficient and low-cost emission control.
en.method: Collected operational data from four cement plants, benchmarked nine ML architectures, incorporated short-term process history, built a 9-minute-ahead NOx overshoot forecasting model, and developed a surrogate model to optimize SNCR operation.
en.result: Short-term history nearly tripled NOx prediction accuracy; surrogate model projects 34-64% NOx reduction with ~$58,000/year NH3 savings.
en.help_to_user: The framework enables emission control without hardware modifications and is transferable to other hard-to-abate industries like steel, glass, and lime.

idea_spark.idea: Combine short-term process memory features with multi-plant data to boost prediction accuracy, and use surrogate models to optimize operational parameters.
idea_spark.risk: Model performance depends on data quality and plant-specific conditions; cross-plant generalization requires further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging process memory (time dependence of NOx formation) for predictive modeling inspires temporal modeling of other industrial emissions.

papers.title: Stability-Driven Motion Generation for Object-Guided Human-Human Co-Manipulation
papers.summary: Co-manipulation requires multiple humans to synchronize their motions with a shared object while ensuring reasonable interactions, maintaining natural poses, and preserving stable states. However, most existing motion generation approaches are designed for single-character scenarios or fail to account for payload-induced dynamics. In this work, we propose a flow-matching framework that ensures the generated co-manipulation motions align with the intended goals while maintaining naturalness and effectiveness. Specifically, we first introduce a generative model that derives explicit manipulation strategies from the object's affordance and spatial configuration, which guide the motion flow toward successful manipulation. To improve motion quality, we then design an adversarial interaction prior that promotes natural individual poses and realistic inter-person interactions during co-manipulation. In addition, we also incorporate a stability-driven simulation into the flow matching process, which refines unstable interaction states through sampling-based optimization and directly adjusts the vector field regression to promote more effective manipulation. The experimental results demonstrate that our method achieves higher contact accuracy, lower penetration, and better distributional fidelity compared to state-of-the-art human-object interaction baselines. The code is available at https://github.com/boycehbz/StaCOM.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于流匹配的框架，用于生成多人协同操作物体的自然、稳定运动。
ai.motivation: 现有运动生成方法多针对单人场景，未考虑负载引起的动力学变化，导致协同操作不自然、不稳定。
ai.method: 利用物体可供性和空间配置推导操作策略，通过对抗交互先验提升个体姿态和人际交互质量，并引入稳定性驱动的仿真优化流匹配过程。
ai.result: 在接触精度、穿透率和分布保真度上优于现有基线方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何生成多人协同操作物体的自然运动，可用于机器人协作或虚拟现实中的交互设计。

idea_spark.idea: 将物体可供性作为显式引导，结合对抗训练和物理仿真，实现更鲁棒的协同运动生成。
idea_spark.risk: 对抗训练可能引入不稳定梯度，仿真与真实环境存在差距，需进一步验证泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从物体可供性出发设计操作策略，启发我们思考如何利用环境约束简化复杂交互建模。

en.tldr: A flow-matching framework for generating natural and stable motions in human-human co-manipulation of objects.
en.motivation: Existing motion generation methods focus on single-character scenarios and neglect payload-induced dynamics, leading to unnatural and unstable co-manipulation.
en.method: Derives manipulation strategies from object affordance and spatial configuration, uses adversarial interaction prior for natural poses and interactions, and incorporates stability-driven simulation to refine flow matching.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art baselines in contact accuracy, penetration, and distribution fidelity.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how to generate natural co-manipulation motions, useful for robot collaboration or VR interaction design.

idea_spark.idea: Using object affordance as explicit guidance with adversarial training and physics simulation for robust co-motion generation.
idea_spark.risk: Adversarial training may cause gradient instability; simulation-to-reality gap needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging object affordance to design manipulation strategies inspires simplifying complex interaction modeling via environmental constraints.

papers.title: Sharp global and almost everywhere convergence rates for periodic homogenization of viscous quadratic Hamilton-Jacobi equations
papers.summary: We study the periodic homogenization of the viscous Hamilton--Jacobi equation   \[ u_t^\varepsilon + \frac{1}{2}|Du^\varepsilon|^2 + V\!\left(\frac{x}{\varepsilon}\right) = \frac{\varepsilon}{2}Δu^\varepsilon \qquad \text{in } \mathbb{R}^n \times (0,\infty), \]   with initial datum $g \in W^{1,\infty}(\mathbb{R}^n)$, where $V$ is Lipschitz continuous and $\mathbb{Z}^n$-periodic. We prove the sharp global estimate   \[ |u^\varepsilon(x,t)-u(x,t)| \leq \varepsilon\!\left(C+\frac{n}{2}\log\!\left(\frac{\max\{t,\varepsilon\}}{\varepsilon}\right)\right) \qquad \text{for all } (x,t)\in \mathbb{R}^n \times [0,\infty), \]   where $\varepsilon \in (0,1]$, $u$ solves the limiting (homogenized) equation and $C>0$ is a constant depending only on $\|Dg\|_{L^\infty(\mathbb{R}^n)}$, $\|DV\|_{L^\infty(\mathbb{R}^n)}$, and $n$.   We further show that if $g$ is locally semiconcave, then   \[|u^\varepsilon(x,t)-u(x,t)| \leq C_{x,t}\varepsilon \qquad \text{for a.e. } (x,t)\in \mathbb{R}^n \times (0,\infty),\]   where $C_{x,t}$ depends on $(x,t)$, $\|Dg\|_{L^\infty(\mathbb{R}^n)}$, and $\|DV\|_{L^\infty(\mathbb{R}^n)}$. More precisely, the above improved rate holds at every point $(x,t)$ where $u(\cdot,t)$ is twice differentiable at $x$. In particular, this occurs for a.e. $x\in \mathbb{R}^n$, since $u(\cdot,t)$ is locally semiconcave.   We conclude by raising the open problem of whether the same $O(\varepsilon |\log \varepsilon|)$ rate remains valid for general strictly convex Hamiltonians or general periodic diffusions.

ai.tldr: 研究了粘性Hamilton-Jacobi方程在周期均匀化中的全局和几乎处处收敛速率，得到了尖锐的O(ε log ε)估计。
ai.motivation: 为粘性Hamilton-Jacobi方程的周期均匀化提供精确的收敛速率，填补理论空白。
ai.method: 利用粘性解方法、半凹性分析和精细的误差估计技术。
ai.result: 证明了全局O(ε log ε)收敛速率，并在初始数据半凹时得到几乎处处O(ε)速率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解粘性Hamilton-Jacobi方程均匀化中的收敛行为，为数值模拟提供理论依据。

idea_spark.idea: 利用半凹性改进局部收敛速率，可能适用于更一般的凸Hamilton量或周期扩散。
idea_spark.risk: 推广到一般严格凸Hamilton量时，对数项可能无法消除。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从半凹性出发，探索解的正则性与收敛速率之间的内在联系。

en.tldr: Sharp global O(ε log ε) and almost everywhere O(ε) convergence rates are established for periodic homogenization of viscous quadratic Hamilton-Jacobi equations.
en.motivation: To provide precise convergence rates for periodic homogenization of viscous Hamilton-Jacobi equations, filling a theoretical gap.
en.method: Viscosity solution methods, semiconcavity analysis, and refined error estimates.
en.result: Global O(ε log ε) rate is proved, and an improved O(ε) rate holds almost everywhere when the initial data is semiconcave.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand convergence behavior in homogenization of viscous Hamilton-Jacobi equations, providing theoretical support for numerical simulations.

idea_spark.idea: Using semiconcavity to improve local convergence rates may apply to general convex Hamiltonians or periodic diffusions.
idea_spark.risk: The logarithmic factor may not be removable for general strictly convex Hamiltonians.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explore the intrinsic link between solution regularity (semiconcavity) and convergence rates.

papers.title: Relative Entropy Estimation in Function Space: Theory and Applications to Trajectory Inference
papers.summary: Trajectory Inference (TI) seeks to recover latent dynamical processes from snapshot data, where only independent samples from time-indexed marginals are observed. In applications such as single-cell genomics, destructive measurements make path-space laws non-identifiable from finitely many marginals, leaving held-out marginal prediction as the dominant but limited evaluation protocol. We introduce a general framework for estimating the Kullback-Leibler divergence (KL) divergence between probability measures on function space, yielding a tractable, data-driven estimator that is scalable to realistic snapshot datasets. We validate the accuracy of our estimator on a benchmark suite, where the estimated functional KL closely matches the analytic KL. Applying this framework to synthetic and real scRNA-seq datasets, we show that current evaluation metrics often give inconsistent assessments, whereas path-space KL enables a coherent comparison of trajectory inference methods and exposes discrepancies in inferred dynamics, especially in regions with sparse or missing data. These results support functional KL as a principled criterion for evaluating trajectory inference under partial observability.

ai.tldr: 提出一种在函数空间上估计KL散度的框架，用于轨迹推断方法评估，解决快照数据中路径空间不可识别问题。
ai.motivation: 轨迹推断中，破坏性测量导致路径空间分布不可识别，现有评估仅依赖留出边际预测，缺乏一致性。
ai.method: 构建函数空间上KL散度的可计算数据驱动估计器，适用于大规模快照数据集。
ai.result: 在基准测试中估计值与解析值高度吻合；在合成和真实单细胞RNA-seq数据上，路径空间KL能揭示现有评估指标的不一致，并暴露稀疏或缺失数据区域的推断差异。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种更可靠的轨迹推断方法评估准则，尤其适用于部分可观测场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将路径空间KL作为损失函数，直接优化轨迹推断模型，可能提升推断准确性。
idea_spark.risk: 高维函数空间估计的计算复杂度可能限制实际应用规模。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合最优传输理论，进一步利用函数空间几何结构改进估计效率。

en.tldr: Proposes a framework for estimating KL divergence in function space to evaluate trajectory inference methods, addressing non-identifiability of path-space laws from snapshot data.
en.motivation: In trajectory inference, destructive measurements make path-space laws non-identifiable, and current evaluation relies solely on held-out marginal prediction, lacking consistency.
en.method: Constructs a tractable data-driven estimator of KL divergence on function space, scalable to realistic snapshot datasets.
en.result: Estimated functional KL closely matches analytic values on benchmarks; on synthetic and real scRNA-seq data, path-space KL reveals inconsistencies in existing metrics and exposes discrepancies in inferred dynamics, especially in sparse or missing data regions.
en.help_to_user: Provides a more principled evaluation criterion for trajectory inference methods under partial observability.

idea_spark.idea: Using path-space KL as a loss function to directly optimize trajectory inference models may improve accuracy.
idea_spark.risk: Computational complexity of estimation in high-dimensional function spaces may limit practical scalability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining with optimal transport theory to leverage geometric structure of function space for more efficient estimation.

papers.title: Indirect Prey-taxis VS a Shortwave External Signal in Multiple Dimensions
papers.summary: We address a short-wave asymptotic for one class of quasi-linear second order PDE systems involving the cross-diffusion described by the so-called Patlak--Keller--Segel law. It is common to employ these equations for modelling the predator--prey community with the prey-taxis that means the interactions of two species of particles or cells or anything else through which the species called "predators" is capable of moving directionally while searching for the other species called "prey." However, we suppose the predators to be sensitive not to the prey density but to a driving signal produced by the prey. Additionally, the production of the driving signal is assumed to be sensitive to the intensity of an external field, which is independent from the community state. This is what we call the external signal. It can be due to the spatiotemporal inhomogeneity of the environment arising from natural or artificial reasons. We assume that the external signal takes a general short-wave form and construct a complete asymptotic expansion for the short-wave solutions with no restrictions on the spatial dimension or kinetics of inter/intraspecific reactions. Further, we apply the short wave asymptotic to studying the stability or instability induced by the external signal following Kapitza' theory for the upside-down pendulum. Applying the general results to some special classes external signals, we get examples of suppressing the taxical transport, examples of robustness of the species equilibrium to the signal or, oppositely, blurring the borderline in the parametric space between the areas of stability and instability of this equilibrium. These results contribute to filling the gap in the literature, since the theory and techniques for the asymptotic integration of systems described above represent a weakly charted area.

ai.tldr: 研究了一类基于Patlak-Keller-Segel定律的捕食-被捕食模型，其中捕食者响应被捕食者产生的短波外部信号而非直接密度，并构造了完整渐近展开，分析了信号对系统稳定性的影响。
ai.motivation: 现有文献对捕食者响应外部信号而非直接猎物密度的模型研究不足，尤其是短波信号在多维空间中的渐近分析尚属空白。
ai.method: 采用短波渐近方法，对无空间维度和反应动力学限制的拟线性二阶偏微分方程组构造完整渐近展开，并应用Kapitza倒立摆理论分析稳定性。
ai.result: 得到了抑制趋化输运、物种平衡鲁棒性以及参数空间稳定/不稳定边界模糊化的实例。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了处理复杂交叉扩散和外部信号驱动系统的数学工具，可用于生态建模和模式形成研究。

idea_spark.idea: 将外部信号视为可控参数，设计人工信号来调控捕食-被捕食系统的空间分布或稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设外部信号独立于系统状态，实际环境中信号可能受反馈影响，需验证简化假设的合理性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴Kapitza倒立摆的稳定性机制，启发利用高频外部扰动来稳定原本不稳定的生态平衡。

idea_spark.idea: 将外部信号视为可控参数，设计人工信号来调控捕食-被捕食系统的空间分布或稳定性。
idea_spark.risk: 模型假设外部信号独立于系统状态，实际环境中信号可能受反馈影响，需验证简化假设的合理性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴Kapitza倒立摆的稳定性机制，启发利用高频外部扰动来稳定原本不稳定的生态平衡。

en.tldr: We study a predator-prey model based on the Patlak-Keller-Segel law, where predators respond to a short-wave external signal produced by prey rather than prey density, construct a complete asymptotic expansion, and analyze signal-induced stability effects.
en.motivation: The literature lacks analysis of models where predators respond to an external signal instead of direct prey density, especially short-wave asymptotics in multiple dimensions.
en.method: We use short-wave asymptotic methods to construct a complete asymptotic expansion for quasi-linear second-order PDE systems without restrictions on spatial dimension or kinetics, and apply Kapitza's theory for stability analysis.
en.result: We obtain examples of suppressed taxis transport, robustness of species equilibrium, and blurring of stability-instability boundaries in parameter space.
en.help_to_user: Provides mathematical tools for analyzing cross-diffusion systems with external signals, applicable to ecological modeling and pattern formation.

idea_spark.idea: Treat the external signal as a controllable parameter to design artificial signals that regulate spatial distribution or stability of predator-prey systems.
idea_spark.risk: The model assumes the external signal is independent of system state; in real environments, feedback may occur, requiring validation of simplifying assumptions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by Kapitza's inverted pendulum, we suggest using high-frequency external perturbations to stabilize otherwise unstable ecological equilibria.

papers.title: SMART: A Spectral Transfer Approach to Multi-Task Learning
papers.summary: Multi-task learning is effective for related applications, but its performance can deteriorate when the target sample size is small. Transfer learning can borrow strength from related studies; yet, many existing methods rely on restrictive bounded-difference assumptions between the source and target models. We propose SMART, a spectral transfer method for multi-task linear regression that instead assumes spectral similarity: the target left and right singular subspaces lie within the corresponding source subspaces and are sparsely aligned with the source singular bases. Such an assumption is natural when studies share latent structures and enables transfer beyond the bounded-difference settings. SMART estimates the target coefficient matrix through structured regularization that incorporates spectral information from a source study. Importantly, it requires only a fitted source model rather than the raw source data, making it useful when data sharing is limited. Although the optimization problem is nonconvex, we develop a practical ADMM-based algorithm. We establish general, non-asymptotic error bounds and a minimax lower bound in the noiseless-source regime. Under additional regularity conditions, these results yield near-minimax Frobenius error rates up to logarithmic factors. Simulations confirm improved estimation accuracy and robustness to negative transfer, and analysis of multi-modal single-cell data demonstrates better predictive performance. The Python implementation of SMART, along with the code to reproduce all experiments in this paper, is publicly available at https://github.com/boxinz17/smart.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于谱相似性的多任务迁移学习方法，仅需源模型而非原始数据，适用于小样本目标场景。
ai.motivation: 现有迁移学习假设源与目标模型差异有界，限制性强；且多任务学习在目标样本量小时性能下降。
ai.method: 假设目标左右奇异子空间包含于源子空间且稀疏对齐，通过结构化正则化融入源谱信息，并设计ADMM算法求解非凸优化。
ai.result: 建立非渐近误差界与极小化下界，在无噪声源下达到近最优Frobenius误差率；模拟与单细胞数据分析验证了准确性与鲁棒性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需原始源数据、仅需源模型的多任务迁移学习框架，提升小样本目标任务的预测性能。

idea_spark.idea: 将源模型的奇异值分解信息作为正则化项，引导目标模型学习共享的低维结构。
idea_spark.risk: 若源与目标谱子空间不满足包含关系，可能导致负迁移；非凸优化可能陷入局部最优。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用谱分解实现知识迁移，避免直接数据共享，适用于隐私敏感或数据受限领域。

en.tldr: Proposes a spectral similarity-based multi-task transfer learning method that requires only a fitted source model, not raw data, and is effective for small target sample sizes.
en.motivation: Existing transfer learning relies on restrictive bounded-difference assumptions, and multi-task learning degrades with small target samples.
en.method: Assumes target singular subspaces are contained in source subspaces with sparse alignment; uses structured regularization incorporating source spectral information and an ADMM algorithm for the nonconvex optimization.
en.result: Establishes non-asymptotic error bounds and a minimax lower bound, achieving near-optimal Frobenius error rates under noiseless source; simulations and single-cell data analysis show improved accuracy and robustness.
en.help_to_user: Provides a transfer learning framework that uses only a fitted source model (no raw data) to improve prediction on small-target tasks.

idea_spark.idea: Use singular value decomposition information from the source model as a regularizer to guide the target model toward shared low-dimensional structures.
idea_spark.risk: If source and target spectral subspaces do not satisfy the containment assumption, negative transfer may occur; nonconvex optimization may yield local optima.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage spectral decomposition for knowledge transfer without direct data sharing, suitable for privacy-sensitive or data-limited domains.

papers.title: Structured perturbations of tridiagonal twisted Toeplitz matrices
papers.summary: Twisted Toeplitz matrices constitute a generalization of Toeplitz matrices in the sense that the entries on each diagonal no longer need to be constant, but are given by the values of a continuous function on a partition of $[0,1]$. We study the limiting statistical distribution of the eigenvalues of matrices of the form $R_n(a) = T_n(a) + σ_n X_n$, where $T_n(a)$ is a sequence of non-Hermitian tridiagonal twisted Toeplitz matrices, $X_n$ is a sequence of tridiagonal random matrices whose entries have mean $0$ and finite variance, and $σ_n\to0$. The limiting distribution turns out to be a two-dimensional measure which is in general different from the push-forward of the Lebesgue measure by the symbol. We also explain how the results could extend to banded non-Hermitian twisted Toeplitz matrices.

ai.tldr: 研究三对角扭曲Toeplitz矩阵在结构化随机扰动下的特征值极限分布，发现极限分布不同于符号的勒贝格测度前推。
ai.motivation: 推广经典Toeplitz矩阵的谱理论到扭曲情形，并理解随机扰动对非Hermitian矩阵特征值统计的影响。
ai.method: 考虑形如R_n(a)=T_n(a)+σ_n X_n的矩阵序列，其中T_n(a)为非Hermitian三对角扭曲Toeplitz矩阵，X_n为均值为零、方差有限的三对角随机矩阵，σ_n→0。分析特征值的极限二维测度。
ai.result: 极限分布是二维测度，通常不同于由符号前推的勒贝格测度。
ai.help_to_user: 为理解非Hermitian Toeplitz类矩阵在随机扰动下的谱行为提供理论框架。

idea_spark.idea: 探索更一般随机矩阵（如非三对角）对谱分布的影响，或考虑相关噪声结构。
idea_spark.risk: 极限测度的显式表达式可能复杂，且高维情形下数值验证困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合自由概率论或算子代数方法，可能得到更简洁的极限分布描述。

idea_spark.inspiration: 结合自由概率论或算子代数方法，可能得到更简洁的极限分布描述。

en.tldr: Studies the limiting eigenvalue distribution of tridiagonal twisted Toeplitz matrices under structured random perturbations, finding a 2D measure generally different from the push-forward of Lebesgue measure by the symbol.
en.motivation: Extend spectral theory of classical Toeplitz matrices to twisted variants and understand the effect of random perturbations on non-Hermitian eigenvalue statistics.
en.method: Considers matrix sequences R_n(a)=T_n(a)+σ_n X_n, where T_n(a) are non-Hermitian tridiagonal twisted Toeplitz matrices, X_n are tridiagonal random matrices with zero mean and finite variance, and σ_n→0. Analyzes the limiting 2D spectral measure.
en.result: The limiting distribution is a 2D measure generally different from the push-forward of Lebesgue measure by the symbol.
en.help_to_user: Provides a theoretical framework for understanding the spectral behavior of non-Hermitian Toeplitz-like matrices under random perturbations.

idea_spark.idea: Explore the effect of more general random matrices (e.g., non-tridiagonal) on spectral distribution, or consider correlated noise structures.
idea_spark.risk: Explicit expressions for the limiting measure may be complicated, and numerical verification in higher dimensions could be challenging.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combine free probability or operator algebraic methods to obtain a more concise description of the limiting distribution.

papers.title: Determining metrics from the scattering map of the time-dependent Schrödinger equation
papers.summary: For a time dependent Schrödinger equation, the scattering map is the map sending the asymptotic profile of solution as $t\to-\infty$ to its asymptotic profile as $t\to+\infty$. In this paper we show that, for certain class of metrics, the scattering maps associated to two Schrödinger operators with two time dependent metrics only differ by a compact operator if and only if these two metrics are related by a pull-back of a diffeomorphism.

ai.tldr: 本文研究了一类含时薛定谔方程的散射映射，证明了两个含时度量对应的散射映射仅差一个紧算子当且仅当它们通过微分同胚的拉回相关联。
ai.motivation: 建立含时度量与散射映射之间的精确对应关系，为逆散射问题提供理论基础。
ai.method: 利用散射映射的渐近性质，分析紧算子差异与度量拉回变换的等价性。
ai.result: 给出了含时度量在散射映射下可区分性的充要条件。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解含时薛定谔方程中度量变换对散射行为的影响，可用于量子系统辨识。

idea_spark.idea: 利用散射映射的紧算子差异设计数值算法，从散射数据反演含时度量。
idea_spark.risk: 紧算子条件在实际数值计算中可能难以精确验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将微分几何的拉回概念引入散射理论，为逆问题提供新视角。

zh.tldr: 本文研究了一类含时薛定谔方程的散射映射，证明了两个含时度量对应的散射映射仅差一个紧算子当且仅当它们通过微分同胚的拉回相关联。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将微分几何的拉回概念引入散射理论，为逆问题提供新视角。

en.tldr: This paper studies the scattering map of a time-dependent Schrödinger equation and proves that two time-dependent metrics yield scattering maps differing by a compact operator if and only if the metrics are related by a pull-back of a diffeomorphism.
en.motivation: To establish a precise correspondence between time-dependent metrics and scattering maps, providing a theoretical foundation for inverse scattering problems.
en.method: Analyzing the asymptotic properties of the scattering map and proving the equivalence between compact operator difference and metric pull-back by a diffeomorphism.
en.result: A necessary and sufficient condition for distinguishability of time-dependent metrics via scattering maps.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how metric transformations affect scattering behavior in time-dependent Schrödinger equations, useful for quantum system identification.

idea_spark.idea: Design numerical algorithms using compact operator differences to invert time-dependent metrics from scattering data.
idea_spark.risk: The compact operator condition may be difficult to verify exactly in numerical computations.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introducing the differential geometric concept of pull-back into scattering theory offers a new perspective for inverse problems.

papers.title: Surrogate modeling for interpreting black-box LLMs in medical predictions
papers.summary: Large language models (LLMs), trained on vast datasets, encode extensive real-world knowledge within their parameters, yet their black-box nature obscures the mechanisms and extent of this encoding. Surrogate modeling, which uses simplified models to approximate complex systems, can offer a path toward better interpretability of black-box models. We propose a surrogate modeling framework that quantitatively explains LLM-encoded knowledge. For a specific hypothesis derived from domain knowledge, this framework approximates the latent LLM knowledge space using observable elements (input-output pairs) through extensive prompting across a comprehensive range of simulated scenarios. Through proof-of-concept experiments in medical predictions, we demonstrate our framework's effectiveness in revealing the extent to which LLMs "perceive" each input variable in relation to the output. Particularly, given concerns that LLMs may perpetuate inaccuracies and societal biases embedded in their training data, our experiments using this framework quantitatively revealed both associations that contradict established medical knowledge and the persistence of scientifically refuted racial assumptions within LLM-encoded knowledge. By disclosing these issues, our framework can act as a red-flag indicator to support the safe and reliable application of these models.

ai.tldr: 提出一种代理建模框架，通过大量提示和模拟场景，定量揭示大型语言模型在医学预测中编码的知识，包括与医学常识矛盾的关系和种族偏见。
ai.motivation: 大型语言模型的黑箱特性使其在医学预测中的知识编码机制不透明，且可能包含训练数据中的不准确性和社会偏见，需要可解释性方法。
ai.method: 基于领域知识的假设，通过广泛提示模拟场景，用可观测的输入-输出对近似LLM的潜在知识空间，定量评估每个输入变量对输出的感知程度。
ai.result: 在医学预测概念验证实验中，框架有效揭示了LLM中与已知医学知识相悖的关联，以及科学上已被驳斥的种族假设的持续存在。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户识别LLM在医学预测中的潜在错误和偏见，作为安全应用的预警指标。

idea_spark.idea: 结合因果推断，进一步区分LLM编码的相关性与因果关系。
idea_spark.risk: 代理模型可能无法完全捕捉LLM的复杂行为，导致解释不完整或误导。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用代理模型作为“红牌”机制，在模型部署前自动检测有害偏见。

idea_spark.risk: 代理模型可能无法完全捕捉LLM的复杂行为，导致解释不完整或误导。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用代理模型作为“红牌”机制，在模型部署前自动检测有害偏见。

en.tldr: Proposes a surrogate modeling framework that uses extensive prompting across simulated scenarios to quantitatively reveal how LLMs encode knowledge in medical predictions, including contradictions to established medical knowledge and persistent racial biases.
en.motivation: The black-box nature of LLMs obscures their knowledge encoding mechanisms, and they may perpetuate inaccuracies and societal biases from training data, necessitating interpretability methods.
en.method: Based on domain-specific hypotheses, the framework approximates the latent LLM knowledge space using observable input-output pairs through extensive prompting across a comprehensive range of simulated scenarios, quantifying each input variable's perceived relation to the output.
en.result: Proof-of-concept experiments in medical predictions effectively revealed associations contradicting established medical knowledge and the persistence of scientifically refuted racial assumptions within LLM-encoded knowledge.
en.help_to_user: Helps users identify potential errors and biases in LLM-based medical predictions, serving as a red-flag indicator for safe deployment.

idea_spark.idea: Integrate causal inference to distinguish correlations from causal relationships encoded in LLMs.
idea_spark.risk: Surrogate models may not fully capture LLM complexity, leading to incomplete or misleading explanations.
idea_spark.inspiration: Use surrogate models as a 'red flag' mechanism to automatically detect harmful biases before model deployment.

papers.title: Weak error for SDEs with additive stable noise and singular drift: choose the test function in the same space as the drift!
papers.summary: We emphasize that for a stochastic differential equation with isotropic stable additive noise and non Lipschitz drift, when considering an appropriate discretization scheme and the associated weak error, it is somehow natural to consider a test function having the same spatial regularity as the drift involved. We will in particular focus on drifts belonging to Lebsegue, H{ö}lder or Besov spaces with negative regularity index in their spatial variable. Choosing such a test function allows to improve the convergence rate previously obtained on the densities (for Lebesgue or H{ö}lder drifts) or preserve the rate for possibly singular generalized test functions (for Besov spaces with negative regularity).

ai.tldr: 针对带有加性稳定噪声和奇异漂移的随机微分方程，选择与漂移具有相同空间正则性的测试函数，可改善弱误差收敛速度。
ai.motivation: 现有方法在处理非Lipschitz漂移时弱误差收敛速度有限，需要更合适的测试函数选择策略。
ai.method: 将漂移置于Lebesgue、Hölder或负正则性Besov空间中，并选取相同正则性的测试函数进行弱误差分析。
ai.result: 对于Lebesgue或Hölder漂移，收敛速度优于基于密度的结果；对于负正则性Besov空间，可保持收敛速度。
ai.help_to_user: 为奇异漂移SDE的数值模拟提供测试函数选取指导，提升弱近似精度。

idea_spark.idea: 探索在更一般的函数空间（如各向异性Besov空间）中建立测试函数与漂移的对应关系。
idea_spark.risk: 实际应用中漂移的正则性可能未知或难以精确刻画，导致测试函数选取困难。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受泛函分析中对偶空间思想的启发，将测试函数视为漂移的“对偶”对象以优化误差分析。

zh.motivation: 现有方法在处理非Lipschitz漂移时弱误差收敛速度有限，需要更合适的测试函数选择策略。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受泛函分析中对偶空间思想的启发，将测试函数视为漂移的“对偶”对象以优化误差分析。

en.tldr: For SDEs with additive stable noise and singular drift, choosing test functions with the same spatial regularity as the drift improves weak error convergence rates.
en.motivation: Existing approaches yield limited weak error convergence rates for non-Lipschitz drifts, necessitating a better test function selection strategy.
en.method: Place drifts in Lebesgue, Hölder, or Besov spaces with negative regularity index, and select test functions of matching regularity for weak error analysis.
en.result: Improved convergence rates over density-based results for Lebesgue or Hölder drifts; rate preservation for Besov spaces with negative regularity.
en.help_to_user: Guides test function selection for numerical simulation of SDEs with singular drifts, enhancing weak approximation accuracy.

idea_spark.idea: Explore establishing correspondence between test functions and drifts in more general function spaces, such as anisotropic Besov spaces.
idea_spark.risk: In practice, the regularity of the drift may be unknown or hard to characterize, making test function selection challenging.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the duality concept in functional analysis, viewing test functions as 'dual' objects to drifts to optimize error analysis.

papers.title: AI models of unstable flow exhibit hallucination
papers.summary: We report the first systematic evidence of hallucination in AI models of fluid dynamics, demonstrated in the canonical problem of hydrodynamically unstable transport known as viscous fingering. AI-based modeling of flow with instabilities remains challenging because rapidly evolving, multiscale fingering patterns are difficult to resolve accurately. We identify solutions that appear visually realistic yet are physically implausible, analogous to hallucinations in large language models. These hallucinations manifest as spurious fluid interfaces and reverse diffusion that violate conservation laws. We show that their origin lies in the spectral bias of AI models, which becomes dominant at high flow rates and viscosity contrasts. Guided by this insight, we introduce DeepFingers, a new framework for AI-driven fluid dynamics that enforces balanced learning across the full spectrum of spatial modes by combining the Fourier Neural Operator with a Deep Operator Network to predict the spatiotemporal evolution of viscous fingers. By conditioning on both time and viscosity contrast, DeepFingers learns mappings between successive concentration fields across regimes. The framework accurately captures tip splitting, finger merging, and channel formation while preserving global metrics of mixing. The results open a new research direction to investigate fundamental limitations in AI models of physical systems.

ai.tldr: 首次系统证明AI流体动力学模型存在幻觉现象，表现为违反守恒定律的虚假界面和反向扩散，根源在于谱偏差。
ai.motivation: 不稳定流动的AI建模因多尺度指进模式难以精确解析而面临挑战，需识别并解决模型幻觉问题。
ai.method: 提出DeepFingers框架，结合傅里叶神经算子和深度算子网络，在时间和粘度对比条件下学习浓度场映射，实现全谱平衡学习。
ai.result: 准确捕捉尖端分裂、指进合并和通道形成，保持混合全局指标，消除物理上不合理的幻觉。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解AI物理模型中幻觉的成因及缓解方法，指导更可靠的流体动力学AI建模。

idea_spark.idea: 将谱偏差约束与物理守恒定律显式嵌入损失函数，开发通用物理AI幻觉检测与校正框架。
idea_spark.risk: 谱偏差校正可能增加计算成本，且对高非线性系统效果需验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受大语言模型幻觉研究启发，将类似概念引入物理AI，揭示谱偏差作为根本机制。

en.motivation: AI modeling of unstable flow remains challenging due to rapidly evolving multiscale fingering patterns; need to identify and address model hallucinations.

idea_spark.idea: Embed spectral bias constraints and physical conservation laws explicitly into loss functions to develop a general hallucination detection and correction framework for physical AI.
idea_spark.risk: Spectral bias correction may increase computational cost, and effectiveness for highly nonlinear systems needs validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by hallucination research in large language models, applying similar concepts to physical AI reveals spectral bias as the root mechanism.

papers.title: Gauge-Equivariant Graph Neural Networks for Lattice Gauge Theories
papers.summary: Local gauge symmetry underlies fundamental interactions and strongly correlated quantum matter, yet existing machine-learning approaches lack a general, principled framework for learning under site-dependent symmetries, particularly for intrinsically nonlocal observables. Here we introduce a gauge-equivariant graph neural network that embeds non-Abelian symmetry directly into message passing via matrix-valued, gauge-covariant features and symmetry-compatible updates, extending equivariant learning from global to fully local symmetries. In this formulation, message passing implements gauge-covariant transport across the lattice, allowing nonlocal correlations and loop-like structures to emerge naturally from local operations. We validate the approach across pure gauge, gauge-matter, and dynamical regimes, establishing gauge-equivariant message passing as a general paradigm for learning in systems governed by local symmetry.

ai.tldr: 提出一种规范等变图神经网络，将非阿贝尔规范对称性直接嵌入消息传递，实现局域规范对称性下的学习。
ai.motivation: 现有机器学习方法缺乏处理局域规范对称性（尤其是非局域可观测量）的通用框架。
ai.method: 通过矩阵值规范协变特征和对称性兼容更新，在消息传递中实现规范协变传输，使非局域关联和环状结构自然涌现。
ai.result: 在纯规范、规范-物质和动力学体系中验证了方法的有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究规范场论、强关联量子物质等局域对称性系统提供了一种新的深度学习范式。

idea_spark.idea: 利用规范协变消息传递模拟非局域关联，可能启发设计更高效的量子模拟器或材料设计模型。
idea_spark.risk: 计算复杂度可能随规范群维度增长，需验证在大规模格点上的可扩展性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将规范对称性嵌入图神经网络，为处理局域对称性提供了通用思路，可推广至其他科学领域（如分子动力学中的局域对称性）。

en.tldr: Proposes a gauge-equivariant graph neural network that embeds non-Abelian gauge symmetry directly into message passing for learning under local gauge symmetries.
en.motivation: Existing ML approaches lack a general framework for handling local gauge symmetries, especially for nonlocal observables.
en.method: Uses matrix-valued gauge-covariant features and symmetry-compatible updates to implement gauge-covariant transport in message passing, enabling nonlocal correlations and loop structures to emerge naturally.
en.result: Validated across pure gauge, gauge-matter, and dynamical regimes.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new deep learning paradigm for systems governed by local symmetries, such as gauge field theories and strongly correlated quantum matter.

idea_spark.idea: Gauge-covariant message passing for nonlocal correlations may inspire more efficient quantum simulators or material design models.
idea_spark.risk: Computational complexity may scale with gauge group dimension; scalability on large lattices needs verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Embedding gauge symmetry into GNNs offers a general approach for local symmetries, applicable to other scientific domains like molecular dynamics.

papers.title: Sheaf Neural Networks on SPD Manifolds: Second-Order Geometric Representation Learning
papers.summary: Graph neural networks face two fundamental challenges rooted in the linear structure of Euclidean vector spaces: (1) Current architectures represent geometry through vectors (directions, gradients), yet many tasks require matrix-valued representations that capture relationships between directions-such as how atomic orientations covary in a molecule. These second-order representations are naturally captured by points on the symmetric positive definite matrices (SPD) manifold; (2) Standard message passing applies shared transformations across edges. Sheaf neural networks address this via edge-specific transformations, but existing formulations remain confined to vector spaces and therefore cannot propagate matrix-valued features. We address both challenges by developing the first sheaf neural network operates natively on the SPD manifold. Our key insight is that the SPD manifold admits a Lie group structure, enabling well-posed analogs of sheaf operators without projecting to Euclidean space. Theoretically, we prove that SPD-valued sheaves are strictly more expressive than Euclidean sheaves: they admit consistent configurations (global sections) that vector-valued sheaves cannot represent, directly translating to richer learned representations. Empirically, our sheaf convolution transforms effectively rank-1 directional inputs into full-rank matrices encoding local geometric structure. Our dual-stream architecture achieves SOTA on 6/7 MoleculeNet benchmarks, with the sheaf framework providing consistent depth robustness.

ai.tldr: 提出首个在对称正定流形上运行的层神经网络，通过李群结构实现矩阵值特征的传播，在分子性质预测任务上达到最优。
ai.motivation: 现有图神经网络受限于欧氏空间的线性结构，无法有效处理矩阵值二阶几何表示，且标准消息传递缺乏边特异性变换。
ai.method: 利用SPD流形的李群结构定义层算子，设计双流架构将秩1方向输入转化为满秩矩阵，并证明SPD值层比欧氏层更具表达能力。
ai.result: 在MoleculeNet的7个基准测试中6个达到最优，且层框架提供一致的深度鲁棒性。
ai.help_to_user: 为需要处理矩阵值几何特征（如分子中原子取向协方差）的任务提供新的建模思路。

idea_spark.idea: 将SPD流形上的层神经网络与物理模拟中的连续介质力学结合，建模材料内部的应力-应变关系。
idea_spark.risk: SPD流形上的计算复杂度较高，可能限制在大规模图上的应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受分子动力学中原子取向协方差矩阵的启发，将二阶统计量作为图节点特征。

zh.help_to_user: 为需要处理矩阵值几何特征（如分子中原子取向协方差）的任务提供新的建模思路。

idea_spark.idea: 将SPD流形上的层神经网络与物理模拟中的连续介质力学结合，建模材料内部的应力-应变关系。
idea_spark.risk: SPD流形上的计算复杂度较高，可能限制在大规模图上的应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受分子动力学中原子取向协方差矩阵的启发，将二阶统计量作为图节点特征。

en.tldr: Proposes the first sheaf neural network operating on the SPD manifold, leveraging Lie group structure to propagate matrix-valued features, achieving state-of-the-art on molecular property prediction.
en.motivation: Existing graph neural networks are limited by Euclidean linear structure, unable to handle matrix-valued second-order geometric representations, and standard message passing lacks edge-specific transformations.
en.method: Defines sheaf operators using the Lie group structure of the SPD manifold, designs a dual-stream architecture to convert rank-1 directional inputs into full-rank matrices, and proves SPD-valued sheaves are more expressive than Euclidean ones.
en.result: Achieves SOTA on 6/7 MoleculeNet benchmarks with consistent depth robustness from the sheaf framework.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new modeling approach for tasks requiring matrix-valued geometric features, such as atomic orientation covariance in molecules.

idea_spark.idea: Combine SPD manifold sheaf neural networks with continuum mechanics in physical simulation to model stress-strain relationships in materials.
idea_spark.risk: High computational complexity on SPD manifolds may limit scalability to large graphs.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by atomic orientation covariance matrices in molecular dynamics, using second-order statistics as graph node features.

papers.title: A Benchmark of 25 Nonlinear Functions with Domain-Induced Discontinuity for Global Optimization
papers.summary: A benchmark of 25 nonlinear optimization problems with domain-induced discontinuity is proposed to support the performance evaluation of global optimization algorithms under feasibility-scarce and structurally discontinuous landscapes. Referred to as the CPC Benchmark (Challenging Problems for Computation), the test suiteconsists of functions that are continuous on their natural domains, while infeasible regions and undefined evaluations are implicitly embedded in the objective, creating substantial challenges for global minimization. Six representative algorithms from diverse methodological paradigms are assessed to examine the structural complexity and discriminative capability of the benchmark. Numerical results show that many functions possess extremely small feasible regions and strong precision sensitivity near feasibility boundaries, complicating initialization, feasibility discovery, and reliable objective assessment. The findings demonstrate that the CPC benchmark provides clear discriminative power across algorithmic paradigms and offers a rigorous, software-oriented testbed for advancing research in global optimization.

ai.tldr: 提出了一个包含25个非线性函数的基准测试集（CPC基准），这些函数在自然域上连续但通过隐式不可行区域和未定义评估引入结构不连续性，用于评估全局优化算法在可行性稀缺和结构不连续景观下的性能。
ai.motivation: 现有基准测试缺乏专门针对域诱导不连续性和可行性稀缺问题的测试集，难以有效评估全局优化算法在复杂现实问题中的表现。
ai.method: 设计了25个连续自然域函数，通过隐式嵌入不可行区域和未定义评估来创建结构不连续的优化问题，并选取六种代表性算法进行测试。
ai.result: 许多函数具有极小的可行区域和强精度敏感性，对初始化、可行性发现和可靠目标评估构成挑战，CPC基准能清晰区分不同算法范式的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 为研究全局优化算法的用户提供了一个严格、面向软件的测试平台，特别适用于评估算法在可行性稀缺和结构不连续场景下的鲁棒性和效率。

idea_spark.idea: 利用域诱导不连续性作为隐式约束，可启发设计更高效的可行性发现策略或自适应初始化方法。
idea_spark.risk: 基准函数可能过度简化真实问题的复杂性，导致算法在特定测试集上过拟合而失去泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受CPC基准中极小可行区域和精度敏感性的启发，可探索结合局部搜索与全局探索的混合算法，或开发基于代理模型的可行性预测方法。

idea_spark.risk: 基准函数可能过度简化真实问题的复杂性，导致算法在特定测试集上过拟合而失去泛化能力。

en.tldr: Proposes a benchmark of 25 nonlinear functions (CPC Benchmark) that are continuous on their natural domains but embed infeasible regions and undefined evaluations to create structurally discontinuous landscapes, for evaluating global optimization algorithms under feasibility-scarce conditions.
en.motivation: Existing benchmarks lack focus on domain-induced discontinuity and feasibility scarcity, hindering robust evaluation of global optimization algorithms in complex real-world problems.
en.method: Designed 25 functions with continuous natural domains, implicitly embedding infeasible regions and undefined evaluations to create structurally discontinuous problems, and tested six representative algorithms.
en.result: Many functions exhibit extremely small feasible regions and high precision sensitivity near feasibility boundaries, complicating initialization, feasibility discovery, and reliable objective assessment; the CPC benchmark clearly discriminates algorithmic paradigms.
en.help_to_user: Provides a rigorous, software-oriented testbed for researchers evaluating global optimization algorithms, especially in scenarios with feasibility scarcity and structural discontinuities.

idea_spark.idea: Domain-induced discontinuity as implicit constraints can inspire more efficient feasibility discovery strategies or adaptive initialization methods.
idea_spark.risk: Benchmark functions may oversimplify real-world complexity, leading to overfitting of algorithms to specific test sets and reduced generalization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the tiny feasible regions and precision sensitivity in CPC, one could explore hybrid algorithms combining local search and global exploration, or surrogate-based feasibility prediction methods.

papers.title: An Explicit Formula for the Benjamin-Ono Hierarchy with Applications to Traveling Waves and Zero-Dispersion Limits
papers.summary: In this paper, we first extend the explicit formula \cite{gerard2023explicit} for the classical Benjamin-Ono equation to each flow of the Benjamin-Ono hierarchy on line. We then use this representation to derive two main applications. First, we obtain a complete classification of traveling wave solutions for all higher-order flows in the hierarchy. Second, we analyze the zero-dispersion limit for the corresponding small-dispersion flows. For every fixed time $t\in\mathbb R$, we prove that, at any time, the solution converges weakly in $L^2(\mathbb R)$ as the dispersion parameter tends to $0$, and we provide a geometric characterization of the limit in terms of an alternating sum, which yields the higher-order analogue of the formula obtained in \cite{miller2011zero}, \cite{Gerard2025small} for the Benjamin-Ono equation.

ai.tldr: 本文将Benjamin-Ono方程的显式公式推广到整个Benjamin-Ono层级，并利用该表示完全分类了所有高阶流的行波解，同时分析了小色散极限下的弱收敛行为。
ai.motivation: 将经典Benjamin-Ono方程的显式公式扩展到其层级中的每个流，以便研究高阶行波解和零色散极限。
ai.method: 通过推广已有的显式公式，得到层级中每个流的表示，并基于此进行行波分类和色散极限的弱收敛分析。
ai.result: 对所有高阶流给出了行波解的完整分类；证明了在固定时间下解在L2中弱收敛于一个交替和形式的几何特征。
ai.help_to_user: 提供了Benjamin-Ono层级中高阶流的结构化理解，以及色散极限的严格数学刻画。

idea_spark.idea: 利用显式公式研究非线性色散方程的高阶行波和极限行为。
idea_spark.risk: 公式推广可能依赖于特定可积结构，通用性有限。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将单方程的显式解结构扩展到整个层级，为复杂系统提供解析工具。

en.tldr: This paper extends the explicit formula for the classical Benjamin-Ono equation to each flow of the Benjamin-Ono hierarchy, yielding a complete classification of traveling wave solutions for all higher-order flows and a weak convergence result for the zero-dispersion limit.
en.motivation: To generalize the explicit formula of the Benjamin-Ono equation to every flow in its hierarchy, enabling the study of higher-order traveling waves and zero-dispersion limits.
en.method: By extending the known explicit formula to each flow of the hierarchy, then using the representation to classify traveling waves and analyze weak convergence in the small-dispersion regime.
en.result: Complete classification of traveling wave solutions for all higher-order flows; proof of weak L2 convergence of solutions to an alternating sum geometric characterization as dispersion tends to zero.
en.help_to_user: Provides a structured understanding of higher-order flows in the Benjamin-Ono hierarchy and a rigorous mathematical description of the dispersion limit.

idea_spark.idea: Use explicit formulas to study higher-order traveling waves and limiting behavior in nonlinear dispersive equations.
idea_spark.risk: Generalization may rely on specific integrable structures, limiting broader applicability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Extending a single-equation explicit solution structure to an entire hierarchy offers analytical tools for complex systems.

papers.title: Towards Event-Aware Forecasting in DeFi: Insights from On-chain Automated Market Maker Protocols
papers.summary: Automated Market Makers (AMMs), as a core infrastructure of decentralized finance (DeFi), uniquely drive on-chain asset pricing through a deterministic reserve ratio mechanism. Unlike traditional markets, AMM price dynamics is triggered largely by on-chain events (e.g., swap) that change the reserve ratio, rather than by continuous responses to off-chain information. This makes event-level analysis crucial for understanding price formation mechanisms in AMMs. However, existing research generally neglects the micro-structural dynamics at the AMMs level, lacking both a comprehensive dataset covering multiple protocols with fine-grained event classification and an effective framework for event-aware modeling. To fill this gap, we construct a dataset containing 8.9 million on-chain event records from four representative AMMs protocols: Pendle, Uniswap v3, Aave and Morpho, with precise annotations of transaction type and block height timestamps. Furthermore, we propose an Uncertainty Weighted Mean Squared Error (UWM) loss function, which incorporates the block interval regression term into the traditional Time-Point Process (TPP) objective function by weighting the uncertainty with homoscedasticity. Extensive experiments on eight advanced TPP architectures demonstrate that this loss function reduces the time prediction error by an average of 56.41\% while maintaining the accuracy of event type prediction, establishing a robust benchmark for event-aware prediction in the AMMs ecosystem. This work provides the necessary data foundation and methodological framework for modeling the discreteness and event-driven characteristics of on-chain price discovery. All datasets and source code are publicly available. https://github.com/yosen-king/Deep-AMM-Events

ai.tldr: 构建了包含890万条链上事件记录的数据集，并提出不确定性加权均方误差损失函数，在AMM生态中实现事件感知预测，时间预测误差平均降低56.41%。
ai.motivation: 现有研究忽视AMM层面的微观结构动态，缺乏细粒度事件分类的数据集和事件感知建模框架。
ai.method: 收集Pendle、Uniswap v3、Aave和Morpho四个代表性AMM协议的链上事件数据，提出UWM损失函数，将区块间隔回归项加权整合到传统TPP目标函数中。
ai.result: 在八种先进TPP架构上，UWM损失函数在保持事件类型预测准确性的同时，将时间预测误差平均降低56.41%。
ai.help_to_user: 提供公开数据集和代码，支持对链上价格发现的离散性和事件驱动特性进行建模。

idea_spark.risk: 链上事件数据噪声大、区块时间不规则，可能影响模型泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将传统金融中的事件研究法引入DeFi，利用链上透明性进行微观结构分析。

papers.title: Concept Graph Convolutions: Message Passing in the Concept Space
papers.summary: The trust in the predictions of Graph Neural Networks is limited by their opaque reasoning process. Prior methods have tried to explain graph networks via concept-based explanations extracted from the latent representations obtained after message passing. However, these explanations fall short of explaining the message passing process itself. To this aim, we propose the Concept Graph Convolution, the first graph convolution designed to operate on node-level concepts for improved interpretability. The proposed convolutional layer performs message passing on a combination of raw and concept representations using structural and attention-based edge weights. We also propose a pure variant of the convolution, only operating in the concept space. Our results show that the Concept Graph Convolution allows to obtain competitive task accuracy, while enabling an increased insight into the evolution of concepts across convolutional steps.

ai.tldr: 提出概念图卷积，直接在概念空间进行消息传递，提升图神经网络的可解释性。
ai.motivation: 现有图神经网络推理过程不透明，事后概念解释无法说明消息传递本身。
ai.method: 设计概念图卷积层，在原始表示和概念表示的组合上利用结构权重和注意力权重进行消息传递，并给出纯概念空间变体。
ai.result: 在保持竞争性任务精度的同时，显著增强了对概念在卷积步骤间演化的洞察。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解图神经网络内部的概念演化过程，提高模型决策的可信度。

idea_spark.idea: 将概念图卷积与自监督学习结合，自动发现任务相关的概念节点。
idea_spark.risk: 概念空间的定义和维度选择可能影响模型性能与可解释性的平衡。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受概念瓶颈模型和注意力机制的启发，将可解释性直接嵌入图卷积设计。

en.tldr: Proposes Concept Graph Convolution, which performs message passing directly in the concept space for improved interpretability of Graph Neural Networks.
en.motivation: Existing GNN reasoning is opaque; post-hoc concept explanations fail to explain the message passing process itself.
en.method: Designs a convolutional layer that operates on a combination of raw and concept representations using structural and attention-based edge weights, with a pure concept-space variant.
en.result: Achieves competitive task accuracy while providing significantly enhanced insight into concept evolution across convolutional steps.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand the internal concept evolution within GNNs, increasing trust in model decisions.

idea_spark.risk: Definition and dimensionality of the concept space may affect the balance between model performance and interpretability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by concept bottleneck models and attention mechanisms, embedding interpretability directly into graph convolution design.

papers.title: $L^2({\mathbb R}) $-Unconditional well-posedness for low dispersion fractional KdV equations
papers.summary: We show that the $ L^2({\mathbb R}) $-unconditional well-posedness, that is well-known for the KdV equation, is shared by KdV type equations with weaker dispersion. This is despite the difference in the nature of these equations, which are quasilinear while KdV is semilinear. More precisely we prove that the low dispersion fractional KdV equation $$ \partial_t u -D_x^α\partial_x u +\partial_x(u^2)=0 $$ is unconditionally globally well-posed in $L^2({\mathbb R}) $ for $α\in ]\frac{55}{38},2] $. Our method of proof combined refined bilinear estimates with the energy method enhanced with Bourgain's type estimates developed in Molinet-Vento (2015).

ai.tldr: 证明了低色散分数阶KdV方程在L2(R)中无条件整体适定，色散指数α∈(55/38,2]。
ai.motivation: KdV方程已知在L2(R)中无条件适定，但低色散分数阶KdV方程是拟线性的，与KdV的半线性性质不同，因此需要研究其适定性。
ai.method: 结合精细的双线性估计与能量方法，并引入Bourgain型估计（Molinet-Vento 2015）。
ai.result: 对于α∈(55/38,2]，方程在L2(R)中无条件整体适定。
ai.help_to_user: 为拟线性色散方程的无条件适定性提供了新结果和方法。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试将α的下界从55/38进一步降低，或推广到更高维空间。
idea_spark.risk: 色散指数过低时，非线性项可能破坏适定性，需要更精细的估计。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用分数阶导数算子的符号特性改进双线性估计。

en.motivation: KdV is known to be unconditionally well-posed in L2(R), but low-dispersion fractional KdV equations are quasilinear, unlike the semilinear KdV, so their well-posedness needs investigation.
en.method: Combined refined bilinear estimates with the energy method enhanced by Bourgain-type estimates (Molinet-Vento 2015).
en.result: For α∈(55/38,2], the equation is unconditionally globally well-posed in L2(R).

idea_spark.inspiration: Exploit the sign properties of fractional derivative operators to improve bilinear estimates.

papers.title: On Uniqueness of Mock Theta Functions
papers.summary: We develop a resurgent approach to the problem of unique continuation of mock theta functions across their natural boundary. The starting point is the representation of the associated Mordell-Appell integrals as Laplace transforms of resurgent functions, which serve as the primary analytic objects.   By rotating the Laplace contour by $π$, i.e. onto the Stokes line, one obtains, in all known cases, the mock-modular relations between the Mordell-Appell integrals and the corresponding unary series in $\hat q=e^{-πi τ}$ and $\hat q_1=e^{-πi (-1/τ)}$.   We then prove that these relations admit a unique solution on the $q$-side, expressed in terms of $q=e^{πi τ}$ and $q_1=e^{πi (-1/τ)}$, with coefficients determined by the corresponding Mordell-Appell integrals. This yields a canonical continuation across the natural boundary, given by a resurgent extension of the classical principle of permanence of relations, and singles out a distinguished family of mock theta functions in each group.   We present a complete analysis for the order 3 and 5 cases (mf3 and mf5). The method extends naturally to higher orders; a general theory will appear in a separate paper.

ai.tldr: 本文通过重正规化方法，证明了模拟θ函数在其自然边界上的唯一延拓性，并给出了阶3和阶5情况的完整分析。
ai.motivation: 解决模拟θ函数在自然边界上唯一延拓的问题，为这类函数提供规范的解析延拓。
ai.method: 将Mordell-Appell积分表示为重正规化函数的拉普拉斯变换，通过旋转拉普拉斯积分路径到Stokes线，得到模拟模关系，并证明这些关系在q侧有唯一解。
ai.result: 建立了模拟θ函数在自然边界上的规范延拓，并区分出每个群中一个特殊的模拟θ函数族。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解模拟θ函数的解析性质，为模形式与重正规化理论的交叉应用提供基础。

idea_spark.idea: 利用拉普拉斯变换和Stokes现象，将边界上的延拓问题转化为唯一解问题。
idea_spark.risk: 高阶情况可能涉及更复杂的模群和重正规化结构，需要更一般的理论框架。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从物理中的重正规化理论（如瞬子求和）获得启发，将数学中的唯一延拓问题与重正规化方法结合。

en.tldr: This paper develops a resurgent approach to prove the unique continuation of mock theta functions across their natural boundary, with complete analysis for orders 3 and 5.
en.motivation: To address the problem of unique continuation of mock theta functions across their natural boundary and to provide a canonical analytic continuation.
en.method: Represent Mordell-Appell integrals as Laplace transforms of resurgent functions, rotate the Laplace contour to the Stokes line to obtain mock-modular relations, and prove these relations have a unique solution on the q-side.
en.result: Established a canonical continuation across the natural boundary and singled out a distinguished family of mock theta functions in each group.
en.help_to_user: Provides insight into the analytic properties of mock theta functions and lays groundwork for applications at the intersection of modular forms and resurgence theory.

idea_spark.idea: Use Laplace transforms and Stokes phenomena to convert boundary continuation into a unique solution problem.
idea_spark.risk: Higher-order cases may involve more complex modular groups and resurgent structures, requiring a more general theoretical framework.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by resurgence theory in physics (e.g., instanton summation), combining mathematical uniqueness with resurgent methods.

papers.title: E-values and sequential power-one tests for monotonicity and unimodality
papers.summary: We develop e-values and e-processes testing the null hypothesis that a distribution over nonnegative integers is monotone, and that a distribution over integers is unimodal given a certain mode. Our e-processes lead to tests of power one under any non-null distribution with a sequence of i.i.d. observations, and consistent set-valued mode estimators that eventually equal the true set of modes. Additionally, we characterize the set of all e-values, and therefore the set of all valid tests, with one monotone and unimodal observation, as well as the most powerful e-value for a fixed alternative. We then show that many of our results can be generalized to continuous random variables, relating them to the existing results in the shape-constrained inference literature.

ai.tldr: 本文针对非负整数分布单调性和整数分布单峰性，开发了e值和e过程，实现了功效为1的检验和一致的模式估计。
ai.motivation: 现有形状约束推断方法缺乏基于e值的序贯检验框架，本文旨在填补这一空白。
ai.method: 利用e值和e过程构造序贯检验，并刻画所有有效检验的集合。
ai.result: 提出的检验在任意非零分布下具有功效1，模式估计量最终收敛到真实模式集。
ai.help_to_user: 为形状约束假设提供了一种新的、可序贯进行的检验工具。

idea_spark.idea: 将e值方法与在线学习结合，实现自适应模式估计。
idea_spark.risk: 连续随机变量的推广可能面临更复杂的分布假设。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受形状约束推断文献启发，将e值理论引入该领域。

en.tldr: This paper develops e-values and e-processes for testing monotonicity of distributions over nonnegative integers and unimodality over integers, achieving power-one tests and consistent mode estimation.
en.motivation: Existing shape-constrained inference lacks an e-value-based sequential testing framework; this work fills the gap.
en.method: Construct sequential tests using e-values and e-processes, and characterize the set of all valid tests.
en.result: The proposed tests have power one under any non-null distribution, and mode estimators converge to the true set of modes.
en.help_to_user: Provides a new sequential testing tool for shape-constrained hypotheses.

idea_spark.idea: Combine e-values with online learning for adaptive mode estimation.
idea_spark.risk: Generalization to continuous variables may involve more complex distributional assumptions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the shape-constrained inference literature, introducing e-value theory into this domain.

papers.title: Covariance Steering of Discrete-Time Markov Jump Linear Systems with Multiplicative Noise
papers.summary: We study a finite-horizon covariance steering problem for discrete-time Markov jump linear systems (MJLS) with both state- and control-dependent multiplicative noise. The objective is to minimize a quadratic running cost while steering the system from given mode-conditioned initial means and covariances to a prescribed terminal mean and covariance. We first show that, without loss of generality, feasible controls may be represented by mode-dependent linear feedback together with feedforward and independent random components, and we highlight that, in contrast to the case without multiplicative noise, a purely affine state-feedback law does not in general suffice. To this end, we introduce a lifted-state formulation that embeds the mean and covariance information into a unified second-moment description, and we prove that the resulting lifted problem is equivalent to the original covariance steering problem formulation. This leads to a lossless relaxation in moment variables and an SDP reformulation for the unconstrained case. We further study chance-constrained covariance steering with ball and half-space constraints on the state and control, derive tractable sufficient convex surrogates, and establish an iterative reference-update scheme to reduce conservatism. Numerical experiments on a finance application illustrate our results.

ai.tldr: 研究离散时间马尔可夫跳变线性系统在乘性噪声下的协方差控制问题，提出提升状态公式和SDP松弛方法，并处理机会约束。
ai.motivation: 现有协方差控制方法未考虑乘性噪声和马尔可夫跳变，实际系统（如金融）需要同时处理这两种不确定性。
ai.method: 将控制律分解为模式依赖的线性反馈、前馈和独立随机分量，通过提升状态公式将均值和协方差统一为二阶矩描述，转化为SDP问题。
ai.result: 无约束情况下得到无损SDP松弛；机会约束下推导出凸代理并设计迭代参考更新方案降低保守性。金融数值实验验证了有效性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供处理乘性噪声和跳变系统的协方差控制框架，可用于金融风险控制等场景。
