en.help_to_user: Provides a generalizable and efficient generative framework that can be readily extended to other image fusion tasks and integrated with unsupervised or blind image restoration algorithms.

idea_spark.idea: Combine unsupervised prior with conditional flow matching, using iterative voting and gradient guidance for robust fusion without handcrafted assumptions.
idea_spark.risk: Two-stage training may accumulate errors; random voting increases computational cost; sensitive to the quality of initial pseudo image.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage continuous generation of flow matching to replace discrete diffusion steps, and reduce dependence on paired data via unsupervised pretraining.

papers.title: Improving Facial Emotion Recognition through Dataset Merging and Balanced Training Strategies
papers.summary: In this paper, a deep learning framework is proposed for automatic facial emotion based on deep convolutional networks. In order to increase the generalization ability and the robustness of the method, the dataset size is increased by merging three publicly available facial emotion datasets: CK+, FER+ and KDEF. Despite the increase in dataset size, the minority classes still suffer from insufficient number of training samples, leading to data imbalance. The data imbalance problem is minimized by online and offline augmentation techniques and random weighted sampling. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method can recognize the seven basic emotions with 82% accuracy. The results demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach in tackling the challenges of data imbalance and improving classification performance in facial emotion recognition.

ai.tldr: 提出一种通过合并三个公开数据集并采用平衡训练策略来提高面部表情识别准确率的方法。
ai.motivation: 现有面部表情识别模型受限于数据集规模小和类别不平衡，导致泛化能力不足。
ai.method: 合并CK+、FER+和KDEF数据集，结合在线与离线数据增强及随机加权采样来缓解数据不平衡。
ai.result: 在七种基本表情识别任务上达到82%的准确率。
ai.help_to_user: 如果你需要处理不平衡数据集或提升表情识别模型的鲁棒性，本方法提供了实用的数据集合并与采样策略。

idea_spark.idea: 尝试引入生成对抗网络合成少数类样本，进一步缓解数据不平衡。
idea_spark.risk: 合并数据集可能引入标注不一致或域偏移，需额外清洗或对齐。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用多源数据融合与自适应采样，可推广到更广泛的情感计算或行为识别场景。

en.tldr: Proposes a method that merges three public datasets and uses balanced training strategies to improve facial emotion recognition accuracy.
en.motivation: Existing facial emotion recognition models suffer from limited dataset size and class imbalance, leading to poor generalization.
en.method: Merges CK+, FER+, and KDEF datasets, and applies online/offline augmentation and random weighted sampling to mitigate data imbalance.
en.result: Achieves 82% accuracy on seven basic emotion recognition tasks.
en.help_to_user: If you need to handle imbalanced datasets or improve robustness of emotion recognition models, this method provides practical dataset merging and sampling strategies.

idea_spark.risk: Merging datasets may introduce annotation inconsistencies or domain shifts, requiring additional cleaning or alignment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leveraging multi-source data fusion and adaptive sampling can be extended to broader affective computing or behavior recognition scenarios.

papers.title: Forage V2: Knowledge Evolution and Transfer in Autonomous Agent Organizations
papers.summary: Autonomous agents operating in open-world tasks -- where the completion boundary is not given in advance -- face denominator blindness: they systematically underestimate the scope of the target space. Forage V1 addressed this through co-evolving evaluation (an independent Evaluator discovers what "complete" means) and method isolation (Evaluator and Planner cannot see each other's code). V2 extends the architecture from a single expedition to a learning organization: experience accumulates across runs, transfers across model capabilities, and institutional safeguards prevent knowledge degradation.   We demonstrate two claims across three task types (web scraping, API queries, mathematical reasoning). Knowledge accumulation: over six runs, knowledge entries grow from 0 to 54, and denominator estimates stabilize as domain understanding deepens. Knowledge transfer: a weaker agent (Sonnet) seeded with a stronger agent's (Opus) knowledge narrows a 6.6pp coverage gap to 1.1pp, halves cost (9.40 to 5.13 USD), converges in half the rounds (mean 4.5 vs. 7.0), and three independent seeded runs arrive at exactly the same denominator estimate (266), suggesting organizational knowledge calibrates evaluation itself.   V2's contribution is architectural: it designs institutions -- audit separation, contract protocols, organizational memory -- that make any agent more reliable upon entry. The accumulated experience is organizational, model-agnostic, and transferable, stored as readable documents that any future agent inherits regardless of provider or capability level.

ai.tldr: Forage V2 将单次探险扩展为学习型组织，通过审计分离、合约协议和组织记忆，实现跨运行的知识积累与跨模型的知识迁移，使任何智能体在进入时更可靠。
ai.motivation: 开放世界任务中智能体存在分母盲视，即系统性地低估目标空间范围。V1 通过协同进化的评估和方法隔离解决，V2 进一步需要跨运行积累经验、跨模型迁移知识，并防止知识退化。
ai.method: 设计机构化架构：审计分离（评估者与规划者互不可见代码）、合约协议、组织记忆（可读文档存储经验）。知识积累通过多轮运行实现，知识迁移通过将强智能体的知识种子注入弱智能体。
ai.result: 六轮运行知识条目从0增至54，分母估计稳定；弱智能体（Sonnet）使用强智能体（Opus）知识后，覆盖率差距从6.6pp缩至1.1pp，成本减半（9.40→5.13美元），收敛轮次减半（平均4.5 vs 7.0），三次独立运行得到相同分母估计266。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种让智能体在开放任务中更可靠、可迁移的架构设计思路，适用于需要长期积累和跨模型协作的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将知识积累与迁移机制与合约协议结合，形成模型无关的组织级经验库。
idea_spark.risk: 知识退化可能源于种子知识不匹配或合约协议过于僵化，需动态调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类组织中的审计与知识管理启发，将机构设计原则引入智能体系统。

zh.tldr: Forage V2 将单次探险扩展为学习型组织，通过审计分离、合约协议和组织记忆，实现跨运行的知识积累与跨模型的知识迁移，使任何智能体在进入时更可靠。

zh.help_to_user: 提供一种让智能体在开放任务中更可靠、可迁移的架构设计思路，适用于需要长期积累和跨模型协作的场景。

idea_spark.idea: 将知识积累与迁移机制与合约协议结合，形成模型无关的组织级经验库。
idea_spark.risk: 知识退化可能源于种子知识不匹配或合约协议过于僵化，需动态调整。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类组织中的审计与知识管理启发，将机构设计原则引入智能体系统。

en.tldr: Forage V2 extends a single expedition into a learning organization with audit separation, contract protocols, and organizational memory, enabling cross-run knowledge accumulation and cross-model knowledge transfer, making any agent more reliable upon entry.
en.motivation: Autonomous agents in open-world tasks suffer from denominator blindness—systematically underestimating the target space. V1 addressed this via co-evolving evaluation and method isolation; V2 further requires accumulating experience across runs, transferring knowledge across models, and preventing knowledge degradation.
en.method: Design institutional architecture: audit separation (Evaluator and Planner cannot see each other's code), contract protocols, organizational memory (readable documents storing experience). Knowledge accumulation via multiple runs; knowledge transfer by seeding a weaker agent with a stronger agent's knowledge.
en.result: Over six runs, knowledge entries grow from 0 to 54, denominator estimates stabilize; a weaker agent (Sonnet) seeded with a stronger agent's (Opus) knowledge narrows a 6.6pp coverage gap to 1.1pp, halves cost ($9.40 to $5.13), converges in half the rounds (mean 4.5 vs. 7.0), and three independent seeded runs arrive at exactly the same denominator estimate (266).
en.help_to_user: Provides an architectural design for making agents more reliable and transferable in open tasks, suitable for scenarios requiring long-term accumulation and cross-model collaboration.

idea_spark.idea: Combine knowledge accumulation and transfer mechanisms with contract protocols to form a model-agnostic organizational experience base.
idea_spark.risk: Knowledge degradation may arise from mismatched seed knowledge or overly rigid contract protocols, requiring dynamic adjustment.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by audit and knowledge management in human organizations, applying institutional design principles to agent systems.

papers.title: Fluctuation theory for spectrally negative Lévy processes killed by additive functionals
papers.summary: In this paper, we study fluctuation identities for spectrally negative Lévy processes killed by a general class of additive functionals. We consider positive co-natural additive functionals (PcNAFs), which include as special cases both absolutely continuous functionals and finite mixtures of local times. Our main result shows that the associated fluctuation identities, such as two-sided exit problems and resolvent measures, retain the same structure as in the classical case and can be expressed in terms of generalized scale functions. These scale functions are characterized as the unique solutions to Volterra-type integral equations driven by Radon measures, thereby extending the results of Li and Palmowski and Li and Zhou. Our approach is based on representing the additive functional as a mixture of local times with respect to its Revuz measure, combined with classical fluctuation identities and an approximation scheme for general Radon measures using Poisson random measures.

ai.tldr: 本文研究被加性泛函杀死的谱负Lévy过程的波动恒等式，证明其结构与经典情形相同，可用广义尺度函数表示。
ai.motivation: 扩展Li和Palmowski、Li和Zhou的结果，处理更一般的加性泛函（包括绝对连续泛函和局部时有限混合）。
ai.method: 将加性泛函表示为关于Revuz测度的局部时混合，结合经典波动恒等式和泊松随机测度逼近方案。
ai.result: 波动恒等式（如双边退出问题、预解测度）保持经典结构，广义尺度函数是Radon测度驱动的Volterra型积分方程的唯一解。
ai.help_to_user: 为谱负Lévy过程在一般加性泛函杀死下的波动理论提供统一框架，便于后续应用。

idea_spark.idea: 利用Revuz测度和泊松随机测度逼近，将一般加性泛函转化为局部时混合，简化分析。
idea_spark.risk: 广义尺度函数的显式求解可能困难，需数值方法或特殊情形。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将加性泛函视为局部时混合的思路，可启发其他随机过程杀死问题的研究。

en.tldr: This paper studies fluctuation identities for spectrally negative Lévy processes killed by additive functionals, showing they retain the classical structure expressed via generalized scale functions.
en.motivation: To extend results of Li and Palmowski and Li and Zhou to a general class of additive functionals, including absolutely continuous functionals and finite mixtures of local times.
en.method: Represent the additive functional as a mixture of local times with respect to its Revuz measure, combined with classical fluctuation identities and an approximation scheme using Poisson random measures.
en.result: Fluctuation identities (e.g., two-sided exit problems, resolvent measures) maintain the classical form; generalized scale functions are unique solutions to Volterra-type integral equations driven by Radon measures.
en.help_to_user: Provides a unified framework for fluctuation theory of spectrally negative Lévy processes under general additive killing, facilitating further applications.

idea_spark.idea: Use Revuz measure and Poisson random measure approximation to transform general additive functionals into mixtures of local times, simplifying analysis.
idea_spark.risk: Explicit computation of generalized scale functions may be challenging, requiring numerical methods or special cases.
idea_spark.inspiration: The idea of representing additive functionals as mixtures of local times can inspire studies of killing problems in other stochastic processes.

papers.title: LLM-guided phase diagram construction through high-throughput experimentation
papers.summary: Constructing phase diagrams for multicomponent alloys requires extensive experimental measurements and is a time-consuming task. Here we investigate whether large language models (LLMs) can guide experimental planning for phase diagram construction. In our framework, a general-purpose LLM serves as the experimental planner, suggesting compositions for measurement at each cycle in a closed loop with high-throughput synthesis and X-ray diffraction phase identification. Using this framework, we experimentally constructed the ternary phase diagram of the Co-Al-Ge system at 900 degree C through iterative synthesis and characterization. We compared two strategies that differ in how the initial compositions are selected: one uses predictions from a domain-specific LLM trained on phase diagram data (aLLoyM), while the other relies solely on the general-purpose LLM. The two strategies exhibited complementary strengths. aLLoyM directed the initial measurements toward compositionally complex regions in the interior of the ternary diagram, enabling the earliest discovery of all three novel phases that form only in the ternary system. In contrast, the general-purpose LLM adopted a textbook-like approach which efficiently identified a larger number of phases in fewer cycles. In addition, a simulated benchmark comparing the LLM against conventional machine learning confirmed that the LLM achieves more efficient exploration. The results demonstrate that LLMs have high potential as experimental planners for phase diagram construction.

ai.tldr: 提出利用大语言模型引导高通量实验构建多元合金相图的方法，并在Co-Al-Ge三元体系中实验验证。
ai.motivation: 传统相图构建需要大量实验测量，耗时费力，亟需智能实验规划方法。
ai.method: 在闭环框架中，通用大语言模型作为实验规划器，每轮建议待测成分，结合高通量合成与X射线衍射相鉴定，迭代构建相图。
ai.result: 成功构建Co-Al-Ge三元相图；领域专用模型aLLoyM优先发现三元新相，通用模型高效识别更多相；模拟基准显示LLM探索效率优于传统机器学习。
ai.help_to_user: 展示了大语言模型在材料科学实验规划中的潜力，可加速相图构建。

idea_spark.idea: 结合领域知识与通用LLM的混合策略，可能进一步提升实验效率。
idea_spark.risk: LLM建议可能受训练数据偏差影响，需谨慎验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将LLM作为实验规划器，启发其他科学领域的自动化实验设计。

en.tldr: Proposes using large language models to guide high-throughput experimentation for constructing multicomponent alloy phase diagrams, experimentally validated on the Co-Al-Ge ternary system.
en.motivation: Traditional phase diagram construction requires extensive experimental measurements and is time-consuming, necessitating intelligent experimental planning.
en.method: In a closed-loop framework, a general-purpose LLM serves as the experimental planner, suggesting compositions for measurement each cycle, coupled with high-throughput synthesis and X-ray diffraction phase identification.
en.result: Successfully constructed the Co-Al-Ge ternary phase diagram; domain-specific model aLLoyM first discovered ternary new phases, while the general-purpose LLM efficiently identified more phases; simulation benchmarks show LLM exploration outperforms conventional machine learning.
en.help_to_user: Demonstrates the potential of LLMs in materials science experimental planning, accelerating phase diagram construction.

idea_spark.idea: A hybrid strategy combining domain knowledge and general-purpose LLM may further improve experimental efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: LLM suggestions may be biased by training data, requiring careful validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using LLMs as experimental planners inspires automated experiment design across scientific domains.

papers.title: Self-Describing Structured Data with Dual-Layer Guidance: A Lightweight Alternative to RAG for Precision Retrieval in Large-Scale LLM Knowledge Navigation
papers.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit a well-documented positional bias when processing long input contexts: information in the middle of a context window receives substantially less attention than content at the boundaries, a phenomenon termed the Lost-in-the-Middle effect (Liu et al., 2024). This limits knowledge-retrieval applications that embed large structured knowledge bases directly in the LLM context. Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) addresses scalability by retrieving only relevant fragments, but introduces substantial infrastructure overhead and is ill-suited to libraries whose semantic boundaries are human-defined rather than statistically learned.   We propose Self-Describing Structured Retrieval (SDSR), a lightweight framework in which structured data files embed human-authored navigational metadata at the file's primacy position, thereby exploiting rather than fighting the LLM's primacy bias. We further propose a Dual-Layer Guidance strategy combining in-file metadata with explicit routing rules in the system prompt.   We validate SDSR through a four-round benchmark using a 190-skill library expanded from 36 to 119 categories via adversarial distractor injection. Four conditions are tested: (A) no guidance, (B) in-file summary only, (C) prompt hint only, (D) both combined. Version D achieves 100% primary routing accuracy (20/20) at 119 categories versus 65% for the no-guidance baseline. We identify a fundamental asymmetry: primary routing is solvable by explicit rules, while secondary cross-category routing requires architectural intent explicitly encoded in the data structure. We further extend SDSR to semi-structured corpora, showing how cross-reference encoding enables operation without vector databases in domains with recoverable document structure.

ai.tldr: 提出一种轻量级框架SDSR，利用文件内元数据和系统提示中的路由规则，克服LLM的中间位置偏差，实现大规模结构化知识库的精确检索。
ai.motivation: RAG基础设施开销大，且不适合语义边界由人工定义的知识库；LLM存在中间位置偏差，直接嵌入大型结构化知识库效果差。
ai.method: 在结构化数据文件的首位嵌入人工编写的导航元数据，结合系统提示中的显式路由规则，形成双层引导策略。
ai.result: 在119类技能库上，双层引导实现100%主路由准确率，基线仅65%；识别出主路由与跨类别路由的不对称性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种无需向量数据库的轻量级结构化知识检索方案，适合人工定义语义边界的知识库。

idea_spark.idea: 利用文件首位元数据和系统提示规则，替代RAG的向量检索组件。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖人工编写元数据，扩展性可能受限；跨类别路由需显式编码架构意图。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用LLM的固有偏差（首位偏差）而非对抗它，设计数据格式和提示策略。

idea_spark.risk: 依赖人工编写元数据，扩展性可能受限；跨类别路由需显式编码架构意图。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用LLM的固有偏差（首位偏差）而非对抗它，设计数据格式和提示策略。

en.tldr: Proposes SDSR, a lightweight framework that uses in-file metadata and system prompt routing rules to overcome LLM's positional bias, achieving precise retrieval in large-scale structured knowledge bases.
en.motivation: RAG incurs high infrastructure overhead and is unsuitable for knowledge bases with human-defined semantic boundaries; LLM's Lost-in-the-Middle effect degrades performance when embedding large structured data directly.
en.method: Embeds human-authored navigational metadata at the primacy position of structured data files, combined with explicit routing rules in the system prompt, forming a dual-layer guidance strategy.
en.result: Achieves 100% primary routing accuracy on a 119-category skill library vs 65% baseline; identifies asymmetry between primary and cross-category routing.
en.help_to_user: Provides a lightweight structured knowledge retrieval solution without vector databases, suitable for human-defined semantic boundaries.

idea_spark.idea: Replace RAG's vector retrieval component with in-file metadata and system prompt rules.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on human-authored metadata, potentially limiting scalability; cross-category routing requires explicit architectural intent.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage LLM's inherent bias (primacy bias) rather than fighting it, by designing data format and prompt strategy.

papers.title: Camera Control for Text-to-Image Generation via Learning Viewpoint Tokens
papers.summary: Current text-to-image models struggle to provide precise camera control using natural language alone. In this work, we present a framework for precise camera control with global scene understanding in text-to-image generation by learning parametric camera tokens. We fine-tune image generation models for viewpoint-conditioned text-to-image generation on a curated dataset that combines 3D-rendered images for geometric supervision and photorealistic augmentations for appearance and background diversity. Qualitative and quantitative experiments demonstrate that our method achieves state-of-the-art accuracy while preserving image quality and prompt fidelity. Unlike prior methods that overfit to object-specific appearance correlations, our viewpoint tokens learn factorized geometric representations that transfer to unseen object categories. Our work shows that text-vision latent spaces can be endowed with explicit 3D camera structure, offering a pathway toward geometrically-aware prompts for text-to-image generation. Project page: https://randdl.github.io/viewtoken_control/

ai.tldr: 提出通过学习参数化视角令牌实现文本到图像生成中的精确相机控制。
ai.motivation: 现有文本到图像模型难以仅用自然语言提供精确的相机控制。
ai.method: 在结合3D渲染图像和逼真增强的 curated 数据集上微调图像生成模型，学习视角令牌。
ai.result: 在保持图像质量和提示保真度的同时，实现了最先进的相机控制精度，并泛化到未见物体类别。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户通过文本提示精确控制生成图像的相机视角。

idea_spark.risk: 可能依赖特定数据集，泛化到极端视角或复杂场景时效果未知。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受3D视觉和扩散模型结合启发，探索参数化视角控制。

en.tldr: Proposes precise camera control in text-to-image generation by learning parametric viewpoint tokens.
en.motivation: Current text-to-image models struggle to provide precise camera control using natural language alone.
en.method: Fine-tunes image generation models on a curated dataset combining 3D-rendered images and photorealistic augmentations to learn viewpoint tokens.
en.result: Achieves state-of-the-art camera control accuracy while preserving image quality and prompt fidelity, and generalizes to unseen object categories.
en.help_to_user: Enables users to precisely control camera viewpoint in generated images via text prompts.

idea_spark.risk: May rely on specific datasets; performance on extreme viewpoints or complex scenes is unknown.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by combining 3D vision and diffusion models to explore parametric viewpoint control.

papers.title: Minimum Energy per Bit of Unsourced Multiple Access with Location-Based Codebook Partitioning
papers.summary: We derive finite-blocklength bounds on the minimum achievable energy per bit over a Gaussian unsourced multiple access (UMA) channel in the presence of heterogeneous path-loss conditions. We consider a setting in which the path loss is known to the users, which enables the use of location-based codebook partitioning [Çakmak et al., 2025]. Through numerical simulations and a large-system analysis based on the replica method, we quantify the performance gain of this strategy relative to the conventional UMA approach in which all users employ a common codebook.

ai.tldr: 针对高斯无源多址信道，在异构路径损耗条件下，推导了每比特最小可达能量的有限块长界，并量化了基于位置的码本分区策略相对于传统公共码本方法的性能增益。
ai.motivation: 传统无源多址方案中所有用户使用公共码本，未充分利用用户位置信息，导致能量效率受限。
ai.method: 利用已知路径损耗进行基于位置的码本分区，结合有限块长界推导和基于复制方法的大系统分析。
ai.result: 基于位置的码本分区策略在能量效率上显著优于传统公共码本方法。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解在异构路径损耗下如何通过位置信息优化无源多址系统的能量效率。

idea_spark.idea: 将用户位置信息作为先验知识用于码本分区，以降低多用户干扰并提升能量效率。
idea_spark.risk: 实际系统中位置信息可能不精确或动态变化，影响码本分区效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发利用环境感知信息（如信道状态、用户分布）进行自适应资源分配。

en.tldr: Derives finite-blocklength bounds on the minimum achievable energy per bit over a Gaussian unsourced multiple access channel under heterogeneous path-loss, and quantifies the performance gain of location-based codebook partitioning over the conventional common-codebook approach.
en.motivation: Conventional unsourced multiple access uses a common codebook for all users, failing to exploit location information and limiting energy efficiency.
en.method: Employs location-based codebook partitioning leveraging known path-loss, combined with finite-blocklength bounds and a large-system analysis via the replica method.
en.result: Location-based codebook partitioning significantly outperforms the conventional common-codebook approach in energy efficiency.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand how location information can optimize energy efficiency in unsourced multiple access under heterogeneous path-loss.

idea_spark.idea: Use user location as prior knowledge for codebook partitioning to reduce multi-user interference and improve energy efficiency.
idea_spark.risk: Location information may be inaccurate or dynamic in practice, affecting codebook partitioning effectiveness.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires adaptive resource allocation leveraging environmental sensing information such as channel state or user distribution.

papers.title: CrackForward: Context-Aware Severity Stage Crack Synthesis for Data Augmentation
papers.summary: Reliable crack detection and segmentation are vital for structural health monitoring, yet the scarcity of well-annotated data constitutes a major challenge. To address this limitation, we propose a novel context-aware generative framework designed to synthesize realistic crack growth patterns for data augmentation. Unlike existing methods that primarily manipulate textures or background content, CrackForward explicitly models crack morphology by combining directional crack elongation with learned thickening and branching. Our framework integrates two key innovations: (i) a contextually guided crack expansion module, which uses local directional cues and adaptive random walk to simulate realistic propagation paths; and (ii) a two-stage U-Net-style generator that learns to reproduce spatially varying crack characteristics such as thickness, branching, and growth. Experimental results show that the generated samples preserve target-stage saturation and thickness characteristics and improve the performance of several crack segmentation architectures. These results indicate that structure-aware synthetic crack generation can provide more informative training data than conventional augmentation alone.

ai.tldr: 提出一种上下文感知的生成框架CrackForward，用于合成逼真的裂纹生长模式以增强数据，提高裂纹分割性能。
ai.motivation: 裂纹检测与分割中标注数据稀缺，传统数据增强方法仅操作纹理或背景，无法生成结构真实的裂纹形态。
ai.method: 结合方向性裂纹延伸与学习到的增厚和分叉，通过上下文引导的裂纹扩展模块（局部方向线索+自适应随机游走）和两阶段U-Net生成器模拟裂纹生长。
ai.result: 合成样本保留目标阶段的饱和度和厚度特征，提升多种裂纹分割架构的性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户生成多样化的裂纹训练数据，提升裂纹检测模型的鲁棒性和泛化能力。

idea_spark.idea: 将裂纹生长建模为方向性延伸与形态学变化，结合随机游走模拟真实传播路径。
idea_spark.risk: 合成裂纹的多样性可能受限于训练数据中的裂纹模式，极端或罕见裂纹形态可能无法覆盖。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受裂纹物理生长机制启发，将结构先验（方向、厚度、分叉）融入生成模型，而非仅依赖像素级纹理合成。

en.tldr: Proposes CrackForward, a context-aware generative framework for synthesizing realistic crack growth patterns to augment data and improve crack segmentation performance.
en.motivation: Scarcity of well-annotated crack data limits detection and segmentation; conventional augmentation only manipulates textures/backgrounds without generating structurally realistic cracks.
en.method: Combines directional crack elongation with learned thickening and branching via a context-guided crack expansion module (local directional cues + adaptive random walk) and a two-stage U-Net-style generator.
en.result: Synthetic samples preserve target-stage saturation and thickness characteristics, improving performance of multiple crack segmentation architectures.
en.help_to_user: Helps users generate diverse crack training data to enhance robustness and generalization of crack detection models.

idea_spark.idea: Models crack growth as directional elongation with morphological changes, using random walks to simulate realistic propagation paths.
idea_spark.risk: Diversity of synthetic cracks may be limited by training data patterns; extreme or rare crack morphologies might not be covered.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by physical crack growth mechanisms, incorporating structural priors (direction, thickness, branching) into generative models rather than relying solely on pixel-level texture synthesis.

papers.title: Using Learning Theories to Evolve Human-Centered XAI: Future Perspectives and Challenges
papers.summary: As Artificial Intelligence (AI) systems continue to grow in size and complexity, so does the difficulty of the quest for AI transparency. In a world of large models and complex AI systems, why do we explain AI and what should we explain? While explanations serve multiple functions, in the face of complexity humans have used and continue to use explanations to foster learning. In this position paper, we discuss how learning theories can be infused in the XAI lifecycle, as well as the key opportunities and challenges when adopting a learner-centered approach to assess, design and evaluate AI explanations. Building on past work, we argue that a learner-centered approach to Explainable AI (XAI) can enhance human agency and ease XAI risks mitigation, helping evolve the practice of human-centered XAI.

ai.tldr: 本文探讨如何将学习理论融入可解释人工智能（XAI）生命周期，提出以学习者为中心的方法来评估、设计和评估AI解释，以增强人类能动性并降低XAI风险。
ai.motivation: 随着AI系统规模和复杂性增长，透明度挑战加剧，需要重新思考解释AI的目的和内容，特别是从人类学习角度出发。
ai.method: 基于已有工作，讨论学习理论在XAI生命周期中的融合，分析以学习者为中心的方法在评估、设计和评估AI解释中的机遇与挑战。
ai.result: 提出以学习者为中心的XAI方法可以增强人类能动性，有助于缓解XAI风险，推动以人为中心的XAI实践演进。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员和实践者理解如何从学习理论视角改进XAI设计，提升解释的有效性和用户理解。

idea_spark.idea: 开发基于学习理论的XAI评估工具，量化解释对用户学习效果的影响。
idea_spark.risk: 过度简化学习理论可能导致解释设计偏离实际用户需求，需谨慎验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴教育心理学中的认知负荷理论，优化AI解释的信息呈现方式。

en.tldr: This position paper discusses infusing learning theories into the XAI lifecycle, advocating a learner-centered approach to assess, design, and evaluate AI explanations to enhance human agency and mitigate XAI risks.
en.motivation: As AI systems grow in size and complexity, the quest for transparency becomes harder, prompting a re-examination of why and what to explain, especially from a human learning perspective.
en.method: Building on prior work, the paper explores integrating learning theories into the XAI lifecycle and analyzes opportunities and challenges of a learner-centered approach for explanation assessment, design, and evaluation.
en.result: A learner-centered XAI approach can enhance human agency and ease risk mitigation, helping evolve human-centered XAI practice.
en.help_to_user: Helps researchers and practitioners understand how learning theories can improve XAI design, making explanations more effective for user comprehension.

idea_spark.idea: Develop learning-theory-based XAI evaluation tools to quantify the impact of explanations on user learning outcomes.
idea_spark.risk: Oversimplifying learning theories may lead to explanation designs misaligned with actual user needs; careful validation is required.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draw from cognitive load theory in educational psychology to optimize information presentation in AI explanations.

papers.title: FluSplat: Sparse-View 3D Editing without Test-Time Optimization
papers.summary: Recent advances in text-guided image editing and 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) have enabled high-quality 3D scene manipulation. However, existing pipelines rely on iterative edit-and-fit optimization at test time, alternating between 2D diffusion editing and 3D reconstruction. This process is computationally expensive, scene-specific, and prone to cross-view inconsistencies.   We propose a feed-forward framework for cross-view consistent 3D scene editing from sparse views. Instead of enforcing consistency through iterative 3D refinement, we introduce a cross-view regularization scheme in the image domain during training. By jointly supervising multi-view edits with geometric alignment constraints, our model produces view-consistent results without per-scene optimization at inference. The edited views are then lifted into 3D via a feedforward 3DGS model, yielding a coherent 3DGS representation in a single forward pass.   Experiments demonstrate competitive editing fidelity and substantially improved cross-view consistency compared to optimization-based methods, while reducing inference time by orders of magnitude.

ai.tldr: 提出一种前馈式框架FluSplat，从稀疏视角实现跨视角一致的3D场景编辑，无需测试时优化。
ai.motivation: 现有方法依赖测试时迭代编辑-拟合优化，计算昂贵、场景特定且易出现跨视角不一致。
ai.method: 在训练时引入图像域的跨视角正则化，联合监督多视角编辑与几何对齐约束；推理时通过前馈3DGS模型将编辑视图提升为一致3DGS表示。
ai.result: 相比优化方法，编辑保真度相当，跨视角一致性显著提升，推理时间降低数个数量级。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户快速从稀疏视角编辑3D场景，无需每场景优化，适合实时或交互式应用。

idea_spark.idea: 利用前馈网络替代迭代优化，将一致性约束从3D域转移到图像域，实现高效3D编辑。
idea_spark.risk: 训练数据需覆盖多种场景和编辑类型，否则泛化性受限；几何对齐约束可能限制编辑自由度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从2D扩散模型的多视图一致性训练中获得启发，将3D一致性隐式编码到前馈网络中。

idea_spark.risk: 训练数据需覆盖多种场景和编辑类型，否则泛化性受限；几何对齐约束可能限制编辑自由度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从2D扩散模型的多视图一致性训练中获得启发，将3D一致性隐式编码到前馈网络中。

en.tldr: Proposes FluSplat, a feed-forward framework for cross-view consistent 3D scene editing from sparse views without test-time optimization.
en.motivation: Existing methods rely on iterative edit-and-fit optimization at test time, which is computationally expensive, scene-specific, and prone to cross-view inconsistencies.
en.method: Introduces cross-view regularization in the image domain during training, jointly supervising multi-view edits with geometric alignment constraints; at inference, edited views are lifted into a coherent 3DGS representation via a feed-forward model.
en.result: Competitive editing fidelity with substantially improved cross-view consistency and orders-of-magnitude reduction in inference time compared to optimization-based methods.
en.help_to_user: Enables fast 3D scene editing from sparse views without per-scene optimization, suitable for real-time or interactive applications.

idea_spark.idea: Replace iterative optimization with a feed-forward network, shifting consistency constraints from 3D to image domain for efficient 3D editing.
idea_spark.risk: Training data must cover diverse scenes and edits for generalization; geometric alignment constraints may limit editing flexibility.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by multi-view consistency training in 2D diffusion models, implicitly encoding 3D consistency into a feed-forward network.

papers.title: Evidence of Layered Positional and Directional Constraints in the Voynich Manuscript: Implications for Cipher-Like Structure
papers.summary: The Voynich Manuscript (VMS) exhibits a script of uncertain origin whose grapheme sequences have resisted linguistic analysis. We present a systematic analysis of its grapheme sequences, revealing two complementary structural layers: a character-level right-to-left optimization in word-internal sequences and a left-to-right dependency at word boundaries, a directional dissociation not observed in any of our four comparison languages (English, French, Hebrew, Arabic).   We further evaluate two classes of structured generator against a four-signature joint criterion: a parametric slot-based generator and a Cardan grille implementing Rugg's (2004) gibberish hypothesis. Across their full tested parameter spaces, neither class reproduces all four signatures simultaneously.   While these results do not rule out generator classes we have not tested, they provide the first quantitative benchmarks against which any future generative or cryptanalytic model of the VMS can be evaluated, and they suggest that the VMS exhibits cipher-like structural constraints that are difficult to reproduce from simple positional or frequency-based mechanisms alone.

ai.tldr: 沃伊尼克手稿的字符序列表现出独特的双向结构约束，且现有生成模型无法同时复现其四个签名特征。
ai.motivation: 沃伊尼克手稿的字符序列长期无法被语言学分析解释，需要系统揭示其结构规律。
ai.method: 对手稿字符序列进行系统性分析，识别出词内右向左优化和词边界左向右依赖的双层结构，并测试两类生成模型（参数化槽位模型和卡尔达诺格栅）能否同时满足四个签名特征。
ai.result: 两类模型在完整参数空间内均无法同时复现所有四个签名特征，表明手稿存在难以通过简单位置或频率机制复现的密码类结构约束。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解沃伊尼克手稿的结构特性，为未来密码分析或生成模型提供定量基准。

idea_spark.idea: 结合深度学习和符号推理，设计能同时满足四个签名特征的新型生成模型。
idea_spark.risk: 手稿可能并非密码，而是自然语言或人工语言，导致模型假设失效。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从手稿中提取的结构约束可启发设计更鲁棒的密码或隐写系统。

idea_spark.inspiration: 从手稿中提取的结构约束可启发设计更鲁棒的密码或隐写系统。

en.tldr: The Voynich Manuscript's grapheme sequences exhibit unique bidirectional structural constraints, and existing generative models fail to reproduce all four signature features simultaneously.
en.motivation: The grapheme sequences of the Voynich Manuscript have resisted linguistic analysis, necessitating a systematic investigation of their structural patterns.
en.method: Systematic analysis of the manuscript's grapheme sequences revealed a dual-layer structure: right-to-left optimization within words and left-to-right dependency at word boundaries. Two classes of generative models (parametric slot-based and Cardan grille) were tested against a four-signature joint criterion.
en.result: Neither model class reproduced all four signatures across their full parameter spaces, indicating cipher-like structural constraints that are difficult to replicate with simple positional or frequency-based mechanisms.
en.help_to_user: Provides quantitative benchmarks for future cryptanalytic or generative models of the Voynich Manuscript.

idea_spark.idea: Design a novel generative model combining deep learning and symbolic reasoning to satisfy all four signature features.
idea_spark.risk: The manuscript may not be a cipher but a natural or artificial language, invalidating model assumptions.
idea_spark.inspiration: Structural constraints from the manuscript could inspire more robust cipher or steganography designs.

papers.title: Physics-Informed Conditional Diffusion for Motion-Robust Retinal Temporal Laser Speckle Contrast Imaging
papers.summary: Retinal laser speckle contrast imaging (LSCI) is a noninvasive optical modality for monitoring retinal blood flow dynamics. However, conventional temporal LSCI (tLSCI) reconstruction relies on sufficiently long speckle sequences to obtain stable temporal statistics, which makes it vulnerable to acquisition disturbances and limits effective temporal resolution. A physically informed reconstruction framework, termed RetinaDiff (Retinal Diffusion Model), is proposed for retinal tLSCI that is robust to motion and requires only a few frames. In RetinaDiff, registration based on phase correlation is first applied to stabilize the raw speckle sequence before contrast computation, reducing interframe misalignment so that fluctuations at each pixel primarily reflect true flow dynamics. This step provides a physics prior corrected for motion and a high quality multiframe tLSCI reference. Next, guided by the physics prior, a conditional diffusion model performs inverse reconstruction by jointly conditioning on the registered speckle sequence and the corrected prior. Experiments on data acquired with a retinal LSCI system developed in house show improved structural continuity and statistical stability compared with direct reconstruction from few frames and representative baselines. The framework also remains effective in a small number of extremely challenging cases, where both the direct 5-frame input and the conventional multiframe reconstruction are severely degraded. Overall, this work provides a practical and physically grounded route for reliable retinal tLSCI reconstruction from extremely limited frames. The source code and model weights will be publicly available at https://github.com/QianChen113/RetinaDiff.

ai.tldr: 提出一种物理信息引导的条件扩散模型RetinaDiff，用于视网膜时间激光散斑对比成像，仅需少量帧即可实现运动鲁棒的高质量重建。

en.tldr: Proposes a physics-informed conditional diffusion model, RetinaDiff, for motion-robust retinal temporal laser speckle contrast imaging from only a few frames.
en.motivation: Conventional tLSCI requires long speckle sequences for stable statistics, making it vulnerable to motion and limiting temporal resolution.
en.method: First, phase-correlation registration stabilizes the raw sequence and provides a motion-corrected physics prior; then, a conditional diffusion model performs inverse reconstruction conditioned on the registered sequence and the prior.
en.result: On in-house retinal LSCI data, the method improves structural continuity and statistical stability over direct few-frame reconstruction and baselines, and remains effective in extremely challenging cases.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how to combine physics priors with diffusion models for few-frame, motion-robust retinal blood flow imaging.

idea_spark.idea: Use a registered physics prior as conditioning for the diffusion model, replacing traditional long-sequence statistical estimation.
idea_spark.risk: Registration quality critically affects the prior; extreme motion may cause registration failure.
idea_spark.inspiration: Embedding physical models into generative inverse reconstruction reduces reliance on large training datasets.

papers.title: Infection-Reasoner: A Compact Vision-Language Model for Wound Infection Classification with Evidence-Grounded Clinical Reasoning
papers.summary: Assessing chronic wound infection from photographs is challenging because visual appearance varies across wound etiologies, anatomical locations, and imaging conditions. Prior image-based deep learning methods have mainly focused on classification with limited interpretability, despite the need for evidence-grounded explanations to support point-of-care decision making. We present Infection-Reasoner, a compact 4B-parameter reasoning vision-language model for chronic wound infection classification and rationale generation. To address the scarcity of expert-labeled wound images with reasoning annotations, Infection-Reasoner is trained using a two-stage pipeline: (1) reasoning distillation, in which GPT-5.1 generates chain-of-thought rationales for unlabeled wound images to initialize wound-specific reasoning in a smaller student model (Qwen3-VL-4B-Thinking), and (2) reinforcement learning post-training with Group Relative Policy Optimization on a small labeled infection dataset to refine classification reasoning. On a held-out heterogeneous wound dataset, Infection-Reasoner achieved 86.8\% accuracy, 86.4\% sensitivity, and 87.1\% specificity, outperforming several strong baselines, including GPT-5.1. Rationale quality was further evaluated using both multimodal large language model (MLLM) judges and wound expert review. Across four MLLM judges, visual-support agreement scores ranged from 0.722 to 0.903, while expert review rated 61.8\% of rationales as Correct and 32.4\% as Partially Correct.

ai.tldr: 提出一个紧凑的视觉语言模型Infection-Reasoner，用于慢性伤口感染分类并生成基于证据的推理。
ai.motivation: 现有方法缺乏可解释性，难以支持临床决策。
ai.method: 两阶段训练：先用GPT-5.1进行推理蒸馏，再用强化学习（GRPO）微调。
ai.result: 准确率86.8%，敏感性86.4%，特异性87.1%，推理质量经专家评估61.8%完全正确。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助临床医生从照片中判断伤口感染，并提供可解释的推理过程。

idea_spark.idea: 将GPT生成链式推理与小型模型强化学习结合，降低对专家标注推理数据的需求。
idea_spark.risk: GPT生成的推理可能包含偏差，需专家验证；模型在罕见伤口类型上泛化性未知。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用大模型生成中间推理来训练小模型，实现高效且可解释的临床AI。

en.tldr: Proposes Infection-Reasoner, a compact vision-language model for chronic wound infection classification with evidence-grounded reasoning.
en.motivation: Prior methods lack interpretability, hindering point-of-care decision making.
en.method: Two-stage training: reasoning distillation using GPT-5.1, followed by reinforcement learning (GRPO) fine-tuning.
en.result: 86.8% accuracy, 86.4% sensitivity, 87.1% specificity; 61.8% of rationales rated Correct by experts.
en.help_to_user: Assists clinicians in assessing wound infection from photos with explainable reasoning.

idea_spark.idea: Combine GPT-generated chain-of-thought with small-model RL to reduce need for expert-labeled reasoning data.
idea_spark.risk: GPT-generated reasoning may contain biases; generalization to rare wound types is unknown.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage large model to generate intermediate reasoning for training small, interpretable clinical AI.

papers.title: LaplacianFormer:Rethinking Linear Attention with Laplacian Kernel
papers.summary: The quadratic complexity of softmax attention presents a major obstacle for scaling Transformers to high-resolution vision tasks. Existing linear attention variants often replace the softmax with Gaussian kernels to reduce complexity, but such approximations lack theoretical grounding and tend to oversuppress mid-range token interactions. We propose LaplacianFormer, a Transformer variant that employs a Laplacian kernel as a principled alternative to softmax, motivated by empirical observations and theoretical analysis. To address expressiveness degradation under low-rank approximations, we introduce a provably injective feature map that retains fine-grained token information. For efficient computation, we adopt a Nyström approximation of the kernel matrix and solve the resulting system using Newton--Schulz iteration, avoiding costly matrix inversion and SVD. We further develop custom CUDA implementations for both the kernel and solver, enabling high-throughput forward and backward passes suitable for edge deployment. Experiments on ImageNet show that LaplacianFormer achieves strong performance-efficiency trade-offs while improving attention expressiveness.

ai.tldr: 提出LaplacianFormer，用拉普拉斯核替代softmax注意力，结合可逆特征映射和Nyström近似，实现高效线性注意力。
ai.motivation: softmax注意力的二次复杂度限制了Transformer在高分辨率视觉任务中的应用，现有线性注意力近似缺乏理论支撑且抑制中程交互。
ai.method: 采用拉普拉斯核作为softmax的替代，设计可逆特征映射保留细粒度信息，使用Nyström近似和Newton-Schulz迭代高效计算核矩阵，并开发自定义CUDA实现。
ai.result: 在ImageNet上达到强性能-效率权衡，同时提升注意力表达力。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种理论扎实、计算高效的线性注意力方案，适用于高分辨率视觉任务和边缘部署。

idea_spark.idea: 探索其他核函数（如Matern核）或自适应核参数以进一步优化注意力表达。
idea_spark.risk: Nyström近似可能在高维特征下精度下降，Newton-Schulz迭代的收敛性需保证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 将核方法中的成熟理论（如再生核希尔伯特空间）引入注意力机制设计。

idea_spark.inspiration: 将核方法中的成熟理论（如再生核希尔伯特空间）引入注意力机制设计。

en.tldr: Proposes LaplacianFormer, replacing softmax attention with a Laplacian kernel, combined with an injective feature map and Nyström approximation for efficient linear attention.
en.motivation: Quadratic complexity of softmax attention hinders scaling Transformers to high-resolution vision tasks; existing linear approximations lack theoretical grounding and suppress mid-range interactions.
en.method: Employs Laplacian kernel as a principled alternative to softmax, designs a provably injective feature map to retain fine-grained information, uses Nyström approximation and Newton-Schulz iteration for efficient kernel computation, and develops custom CUDA implementations.
en.result: Achieves strong performance-efficiency trade-offs on ImageNet while improving attention expressiveness.
en.help_to_user: Provides a theoretically grounded and computationally efficient linear attention solution suitable for high-resolution vision tasks and edge deployment.

idea_spark.idea: Explore other kernels (e.g., Matern) or adaptive kernel parameters to further optimize attention expressiveness.
idea_spark.risk: Nyström approximation may degrade in high-dimensional features; convergence of Newton-Schulz iteration must be ensured.
idea_spark.inspiration: Introduce mature kernel method theories (e.g., reproducing kernel Hilbert spaces) into attention mechanism design.

papers.title: Weighted Knowledge Distillation for Semi-Supervised Segmentation of Maxillary Sinus in Panoramic X-ray Images
papers.summary: Accurate segmentation of maxillary sinus in panoramic X-ray images is essential for dental diagnosis and surgical planning; however, this task remains relatively underexplored in dental imaging research. Structural overlap, ambiguous anatomical boundaries inherent to two-dimensional panoramic projections, and the limited availability of large scale clinical datasets with reliable pixel-level annotations make the development and evaluation of segmentation models challenging. To address these challenges, we propose a semi-supervised segmentation framework that effectively leverages both labeled and unlabeled panoramic radiographs, where knowledge distillation is utilized to train a student model with reliable structural information distilled from a teacher model. Specifically, we introduce a weighted knowledge distillation loss to suppress unreliable distillation signals caused by structural discrepancies between teacher and student predictions. To further enhance the quality of pseudo labels generated by the teacher network, we introduce SinusCycle-GAN which is a refinement network based on unpaired image-to-image translation. This refinement process improves the precision of boundaries and reduces noise propagation when learning from unlabeled data during semi-supervised training. To evaluate the proposed method, we collected clinical panoramic X-ray images from 2,511 patients, and experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms state-of-the-art segmentation models, achieving the Dice score of 96.35\% while reducing boundary error. The results indicate that the proposed semi-supervised framework provides robust and anatomically consistent segmentation performance under limited labeled data conditions, highlighting its potential for broader dental image analysis applications.

ai.tldr: 提出一种半监督分割框架，利用加权知识蒸馏和SinusCycle-GAN伪标签细化，在少量标注数据下实现全景X光片中上颌窦的精确分割。
ai.motivation: 全景X光片中上颌窦分割因结构重叠、边界模糊和标注数据稀缺而困难，现有方法依赖大量像素级标注，成本高且难以推广。
ai.method: 采用教师-学生知识蒸馏框架，引入加权蒸馏损失抑制结构差异导致的不可靠信号；同时设计SinusCycle-GAN基于无配对图像翻译细化教师网络生成的伪标签，提升边界精度并减少噪声传播。
ai.result: 在2511例临床数据上达到96.35% Dice分数，优于现有方法，且边界误差更低，证明在有限标注条件下具有鲁棒性和解剖一致性。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种高效利用未标注数据的半监督分割方案，可降低医学图像标注成本，适用于其他结构重叠的解剖区域分割任务。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多视角投影或3D重建信息进一步改进伪标签质量，或探索自监督预训练增强教师网络能力。
idea_spark.risk: SinusCycle-GAN可能引入生成伪影，需验证其在其他数据集上的泛化性；加权蒸馏损失的超参数敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用无配对图像翻译细化伪标签的思路可启发其他弱监督学习场景，如跨模态分割或域适应。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多视角投影或3D重建信息进一步改进伪标签质量，或探索自监督预训练增强教师网络能力。
idea_spark.risk: SinusCycle-GAN可能引入生成伪影，需验证其在其他数据集上的泛化性；加权蒸馏损失的超参数敏感。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用无配对图像翻译细化伪标签的思路可启发其他弱监督学习场景，如跨模态分割或域适应。

en.tldr: A semi-supervised segmentation framework using weighted knowledge distillation and SinusCycle-GAN pseudo-label refinement achieves accurate maxillary sinus segmentation in panoramic X-rays with limited labeled data.
en.motivation: Maxillary sinus segmentation in panoramic X-rays is challenging due to structural overlap, ambiguous boundaries, and scarcity of pixel-level annotations, hindering model development and evaluation.
en.method: A teacher-student knowledge distillation framework is employed with a weighted distillation loss to suppress unreliable signals from structural discrepancies. SinusCycle-GAN, an unpaired image-to-image translation network, refines teacher-generated pseudo labels to improve boundary precision and reduce noise propagation.
en.result: On a clinical dataset of 2,511 patients, the method achieves a Dice score of 96.35%, outperforming state-of-the-art models with lower boundary error, demonstrating robust and anatomically consistent segmentation under limited labeled data.
en.help_to_user: Provides an efficient semi-supervised segmentation approach that reduces annotation cost, applicable to other anatomical regions with overlapping structures.

idea_spark.idea: Incorporate multi-view projections or 3D reconstruction to further improve pseudo-label quality, or explore self-supervised pretraining to enhance the teacher network.
idea_spark.risk: SinusCycle-GAN may introduce generative artifacts; hyperparameter sensitivity of the weighted distillation loss requires careful tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: The use of unpaired image-to-image translation for pseudo-label refinement can inspire other weakly supervised learning scenarios, such as cross-modal segmentation or domain adaptation.

papers.title: Dual Causal Inference: Integrating Backdoor Adjustment and Instrumental Variable Learning for Medical VQA
papers.summary: Medical Visual Question Answering (MedVQA) aims to generate clinically reliable answers conditioned on complex medical images and questions. However, existing methods often overfit to superficial cross-modal correlations, neglecting the intrinsic biases embedded in multimodal medical data. Consequently, models become vulnerable to cross-modal confounding effects, severely hindering their ability to provide trustworthy diagnostic reasoning. To address this limitation, we propose a novel Dual Causal Inference (DCI) framework for MedVQA. To the best of our knowledge, DCI is the first unified architecture that integrates Backdoor Adjustment (BDA) and Instrumental Variable (IV) learning to jointly tackle both observable and unobserved confounders. Specifically, we formulate a Structural Causal Model (SCM) where observable cross-modal biases (e.g., frequent visual and textual co-occurrences) are mitigated via BDA, while unobserved confounders are compensated using an IV learned from a shared latent space. To guarantee the validity of the IV, we design mutual information constraints that maximize its dependence on the fused multimodal representations while minimizing its associations with the unobserved confounders and target answers. Through this dual mechanism, DCI extracts deconfounded representations that capture genuine causal relationships. Extensive experiments on four benchmark datasets, SLAKE, SLAKE-CP, VQA-RAD, and PathVQA, demonstrate that our method consistently outperforms existing approaches, particularly in out-of-distribution (OOD) generalization. Furthermore, qualitative analyses confirm that DCI significantly enhances the interpretability and robustness of cross-modal reasoning by explicitly disentangling true causal effects from spurious cross-modal shortcuts.

ai.tldr: 提出双因果推断框架，结合后门调整和工具变量学习，消除医学VQA中的可观测和不可观测混杂因素。
ai.motivation: 现有方法过度拟合表面跨模态关联，忽略医学数据中的固有偏差，导致模型易受混杂效应影响，无法提供可信的诊断推理。
ai.method: 构建结构因果模型，用后门调整缓解可观测偏差，用工具变量补偿不可观测混杂，并通过互信息约束保证工具变量有效性。
ai.result: 在四个基准数据集上优于现有方法，尤其在分布外泛化上表现突出，定性分析证实能显式分离因果效应与虚假关联。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过因果推断提升医学VQA的鲁棒性和可解释性。

idea_spark.idea: 探索将工具变量学习与后门调整结合，用于处理更复杂的混杂结构。
idea_spark.risk: 工具变量的有效性假设可能在实际数据中难以满足，需谨慎验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从因果推断角度重新审视多模态学习中的偏差问题，启发设计更通用的去混杂方法。

zh.motivation: 现有方法过度拟合表面跨模态关联，忽略医学数据中的固有偏差，导致模型易受混杂效应影响，无法提供可信的诊断推理。

zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户理解如何通过因果推断提升医学VQA的鲁棒性和可解释性。

idea_spark.risk: 工具变量的有效性假设可能在实际数据中难以满足，需谨慎验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从因果推断角度重新审视多模态学习中的偏差问题，启发设计更通用的去混杂方法。

en.tldr: Proposes a Dual Causal Inference framework integrating backdoor adjustment and instrumental variable learning to eliminate observable and unobserved confounders in Medical VQA.
en.motivation: Existing methods overfit superficial cross-modal correlations, neglecting intrinsic biases in medical data, making models vulnerable to confounding effects and hindering trustworthy diagnostic reasoning.
en.method: Formulates a Structural Causal Model, mitigates observable biases via backdoor adjustment, compensates unobserved confounders via instrumental variable learned from shared latent space, and uses mutual information constraints to ensure IV validity.
en.result: Outperforms existing methods on four benchmarks, especially in out-of-distribution generalization; qualitative analyses confirm disentanglement of true causal effects from spurious shortcuts.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand how causal inference can improve robustness and interpretability in Medical VQA.

idea_spark.idea: Explore combining instrumental variable learning with backdoor adjustment to handle more complex confounding structures.
idea_spark.risk: The validity assumptions of instrumental variables may be hard to satisfy in real data, requiring careful verification.
idea_spark.inspiration: Revisiting bias issues in multimodal learning from a causal inference perspective inspires the design of more general deconfounding methods.

papers.title: Memory-Augmented LLM-based Multi-Agent System for Automated Feature Generation on Tabular Data
papers.summary: Automated feature generation extracts informative features from raw tabular data without manual intervention and is crucial for accurate, generalizable machine learning. Traditional methods rely on predefined operator libraries and cannot leverage task semantics, limiting their ability to produce diverse, high-value features for complex tasks. Recent Large Language Model (LLM)-based approaches introduce richer semantic signals, but still suffer from a restricted feature space due to fixed generation patterns and from the absence of feedback from the learning objective. To address these challenges, we propose a Memory-Augmented LLM-based Multi-Agent System (\textbf{MALMAS}) for automated feature generation. MALMAS decomposes the generation process into agents with distinct responsibilities, and a Router Agent activates an appropriate subset of agents per iteration, further broadening exploration of the feature space. We further integrate a memory module comprising procedural memory, feedback memory, and conceptual memory, enabling iterative refinement that adaptively guides subsequent feature generation and improves feature quality and diversity. Extensive experiments on multiple public datasets against state-of-the-art baselines demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach. The code is available at https://github.com/fxdong24/MALMAS

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于记忆增强的LLM多智能体系统MALMAS，用于表格数据的自动特征生成，通过分解生成过程、路由选择和记忆模块提升特征质量与多样性。
ai.motivation: 传统自动特征生成方法依赖预定义算子库，无法利用任务语义；现有LLM方法特征空间受限且缺乏学习目标反馈。
ai.method: 将特征生成分解为多个智能体，由路由智能体动态激活子集；集成程序记忆、反馈记忆和概念记忆模块，实现迭代优化。
ai.result: 在多个公开数据集上优于现有基线方法。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种自动生成高质量表格特征的方法，减少人工特征工程工作量。

idea_spark.idea: 将记忆模块中的反馈记忆用于在线学习场景，使系统能根据实时预测误差动态调整特征生成策略。
idea_spark.risk: 多智能体协调可能增加计算开销；记忆模块的存储与检索效率需进一步优化。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类专家通过经验积累和分工协作解决复杂问题的启发，模拟了团队合作与知识记忆机制。

en.tldr: Proposes MALMAS, a memory-augmented LLM-based multi-agent system for automated feature generation on tabular data, improving feature quality and diversity through decomposed generation, routing, and memory modules.
en.motivation: Traditional automated feature generation relies on predefined operators and cannot leverage task semantics; existing LLM methods suffer from limited feature space and lack of feedback from learning objectives.
en.method: Decomposes feature generation into multiple agents with distinct responsibilities, activated by a Router Agent; integrates procedural, feedback, and conceptual memory for iterative refinement.
en.result: Outperforms state-of-the-art baselines on multiple public datasets.
en.help_to_user: Provides an automated method to generate high-quality features for tabular data, reducing manual feature engineering effort.

idea_spark.idea: Apply the feedback memory module in online learning settings, allowing the system to dynamically adjust feature generation strategies based on real-time prediction errors.
idea_spark.risk: Multi-agent coordination may increase computational overhead; storage and retrieval efficiency of the memory module need further optimization.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by how human experts leverage experience accumulation and division of labor to solve complex problems, simulating teamwork and knowledge memory mechanisms.

papers.title: LLM StructCore: Schema-Guided Reasoning Condensation and Deterministic Compilation
papers.summary: Automatically filling Case Report Forms (CRFs) from clinical notes is challenging due to noisy language, strict output contracts, and the high cost of false positives. We describe our CL4Health 2026 submission for Dyspnea CRF filling (134 items) using a contract-driven two-stage design grounded in Schema-Guided Reasoning (SGR). The key task property is extreme sparsity: the majority of fields are unknown, and official scoring penalizes both empty values and unsupported predictions. We shift from a single-step "LLM predicts 134 fields" approach to a decomposition where (i) Stage 1 produces a stable SGR-style JSON summary with exactly 9 domain keys, and (ii) Stage 2 is a fully deterministic, 0-LLM compiler that parses the Stage 1 summary, canonicalizes item names, normalizes predictions to the official controlled vocabulary, applies evidence-gated false-positive filters, and expands the output into the required 134-item format. On the dev80 split, the best teacher configuration achieves macro-F1 0.6543 (EN) and 0.6905 (IT); on the hidden test200, the submitted English variant scores 0.63 on Codabench. The pipeline is language-agnostic: Italian results match or exceed English with no language-specific engineering.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于Schema引导推理的两阶段方法，从临床笔记自动填充呼吸困难病例报告表（134项），在稀疏标注和严格输出约束下取得高F1分数。
ai.motivation: 直接从临床笔记填充CRF面临语言噪声、输出契约严格和假阳性代价高的问题，且大部分字段未知，传统单步LLM预测效果不佳。
ai.method: 两阶段设计：阶段1用LLM生成包含9个领域键的SGR风格JSON摘要；阶段2用完全确定性的编译器解析摘要、规范名称、映射到受控词汇、应用证据门控假阳性过滤，并扩展为134项输出。
ai.result: 在dev80上英文宏F1=0.6543，意大利文0.6905；隐藏测试集英文得分0.63，意大利文表现相当且无需语言特定工程。
ai.help_to_user: 提供一种可复用的合同驱动CRF填充框架，适用于高稀疏、多字段的临床表格自动填写任务。

idea_spark.idea: 将阶段1的SGR摘要扩展为包含置信度分数，使阶段2编译器能根据置信度动态调整过滤阈值。
idea_spark.risk: 确定性编译器可能无法处理LLM摘要中的意外格式或语义偏差，导致输出错误。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴编译原理中的中间表示（IR）概念，将LLM视为前端生成IR，后端用确定性规则优化和代码生成。

idea_spark.idea: 将阶段1的SGR摘要扩展为包含置信度分数，使阶段2编译器能根据置信度动态调整过滤阈值。
idea_spark.risk: 确定性编译器可能无法处理LLM摘要中的意外格式或语义偏差，导致输出错误。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴编译原理中的中间表示（IR）概念，将LLM视为前端生成IR，后端用确定性规则优化和代码生成。

en.tldr: Proposes a two-stage schema-guided reasoning approach for automatically filling Dyspnea Case Report Forms (134 items) from clinical notes, achieving high F1 under extreme sparsity and strict output contracts.
en.motivation: Directly filling CRFs from clinical notes suffers from noisy language, strict output contracts, and high false-positive cost; most fields are unknown, making single-step LLM prediction ineffective.
en.method: Two-stage design: Stage 1 uses an LLM to generate a stable SGR-style JSON summary with exactly 9 domain keys; Stage 2 is a fully deterministic compiler that parses the summary, canonicalizes names, maps to controlled vocabulary, applies evidence-gated false-positive filters, and expands to 134-item output.
en.result: On dev80, macro-F1 0.6543 (EN) and 0.6905 (IT); on hidden test200, English score 0.63; Italian matches or exceeds English without language-specific engineering.
en.help_to_user: Provides a reusable contract-driven CRF filling framework suitable for high-sparsity, multi-field clinical form automation.

idea_spark.idea: Extend Stage 1 SGR summary to include confidence scores, enabling Stage 2 compiler to dynamically adjust filtering thresholds based on confidence.
idea_spark.risk: The deterministic compiler may fail on unexpected formatting or semantic deviations in the LLM summary, leading to output errors.
idea_spark.inspiration: Borrow the intermediate representation (IR) concept from compiler design: treat LLM as frontend generating IR, and backend uses deterministic rules for optimization and code generation.

papers.title: Measuring Creativity in the Age of Generative AI: Distinguishing Human and AI-Generated Creative Performance in Hiring and Talent Systems
papers.summary: Generative AI is rapidly transforming how organizations create value and evaluate talent. While large language models enhance baseline output quality, they simultaneously introduce ambiguity in assessing human creativity, as observable artifacts may be partially or fully AI-generated. This paper reconceptualizes creativity as a distributional and process-based property that emerges under shared constraints and competitive incentives. We introduce a quantitative framework for measuring creativity as novelty in synthesis, operationalized through idea generation and idea transformation within embedding space. Empirical evaluation demonstrates that the proposed metrics align with intuitive judgments of creativity while capturing distinctions that surface-level quality assessments miss. We further identify a structural shift toward bimodal distributions of creative output in AI-mediated environments, with implications for hiring, leadership, and competitive strategy. The findings suggest that in the age of generative AI, distinctiveness rather than fluency becomes the primary signal of human creative capability.

ai.tldr: 提出在生成式AI时代，将创造力重新概念化为分布性和过程性属性，并通过嵌入空间中的创意生成与转化来量化新颖性，发现AI导致创意产出呈双峰分布，区分度成为人类创造力的关键信号。
ai.motivation: 生成式AI模糊了人类与AI创造力的界限，传统质量评估无法区分，需要新的框架来测量和区分人类与AI的创意表现。
ai.method: 将创造力定义为在共享约束和竞争激励下的分布性过程属性，通过嵌入空间中的创意生成与转化操作化新颖性，并引入定量指标。
ai.result: 所提指标与直觉判断一致，能捕捉表面质量评估忽略的差异；AI中介环境下创意产出呈双峰分布，区分度而非流畅度成为人类创造力的主要信号。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助招聘和人才系统设计者理解如何区分人类与AI的创意贡献，优化人才评估流程。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个基于嵌入空间相似性分析的创意评估工具，自动识别人类与AI生成内容的创意独特性。
idea_spark.risk: 指标可能被对抗性优化，导致人类刻意模仿AI模式；双峰分布可能加剧对非典型创意的偏见。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受分布性属性启发，可探索将创造力视为动态博弈过程，而非静态产物。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个基于嵌入空间相似性分析的创意评估工具，自动识别人类与AI生成内容的创意独特性。
idea_spark.risk: 指标可能被对抗性优化，导致人类刻意模仿AI模式；双峰分布可能加剧对非典型创意的偏见。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受分布性属性启发，可探索将创造力视为动态博弈过程，而非静态产物。

en.tldr: Proposes reconceptualizing creativity as a distributional and process-based property in the age of generative AI, operationalizing novelty through idea generation and transformation in embedding space, and finds that AI leads to bimodal creative output, with distinctiveness becoming the key signal of human creativity.
en.motivation: Generative AI blurs the boundary between human and AI creativity; traditional quality assessments fail to distinguish them, necessitating a new framework to measure and differentiate human vs AI creative performance.
en.method: Defines creativity as a distributional and process-based property under shared constraints and competitive incentives, operationalized via idea generation and transformation in embedding space with quantitative metrics.
en.result: Proposed metrics align with intuitive judgments and capture distinctions missed by surface-level quality assessments; AI-mediated environments show bimodal distributions of creative output, where distinctiveness rather than fluency signals human creative capability.
en.help_to_user: Helps hiring and talent system designers understand how to distinguish human vs AI creative contributions and optimize talent evaluation processes.

idea_spark.idea: Develop a creativity assessment tool based on embedding space similarity analysis to automatically identify the creative uniqueness of human vs AI-generated content.
idea_spark.risk: Metrics may be adversarially optimized, leading humans to deliberately mimic AI patterns; bimodal distribution may reinforce bias against atypical creativity.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by the distributional property, one could explore viewing creativity as a dynamic game process rather than a static artifact.

papers.title: Graph2Counsel: Clinically Grounded Synthetic Counseling Dialogue Generation from Client Psychological Graphs
papers.summary: Rising demand for mental health support has increased interest in using Large Language Models (LLMs) for counseling. However, adapting LLMs to this high-risk safety-critical domain is hindered by the scarcity of real-world counseling data due to privacy constraints. Synthetic datasets provide a promising alternative, but existing approaches often rely on unstructured or semi-structured text inputs and overlook structural dependencies between a client's cognitive, emotional, and behavioral states, often producing psychologically inconsistent interactions and reducing data realism and quality. We introduce Graph2Counsel, a framework for generating synthetic counseling sessions grounded in Client Psychological Graphs (CPGs) that encode relationships among clients' thoughts, emotions, and behaviors. Graph2Counsel employs a structured prompting pipeline guided by counselor strategies and CPG, and explores prompting strategies including CoT (Wei et al., 2022) and Multi-Agent Feedback (Li et al., 2025a). Graph2Counsel produces 760 sessions from 76 CPGs across diverse client profiles. In expert evaluation, our dataset outperforms prior datasets on specificity, counselor competence, authenticity, conversational flow, and safety, with substantial inter-annotator agreement (Krippendorff's $α$ = 0.70). Fine-tuning an open-source model on this dataset improves performance on CounselingBench (Nguyen et al., 2025) and CounselBench (Li et al., 2025b), showing downstream utility. We also make our code and data public.

ai.tldr: 提出Graph2Counsel框架，基于客户心理图生成合成咨询对话，解决真实数据稀缺问题，提升对话真实性和安全性。
ai.motivation: 心理健康咨询领域因隐私限制缺乏真实数据，现有合成方法忽略客户认知、情绪和行为间的结构依赖，导致心理不一致。
ai.method: 构建客户心理图编码思想、情绪和行为关系，采用结构化提示流水线（含CoT和多智能体反馈）生成咨询对话。
ai.result: 生成760个会话，专家评估在特异性、咨询师能力、真实性、对话流畅性和安全性上优于先前数据集，微调开源模型提升下游基准性能。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员快速生成高质量、心理一致的合成咨询数据，用于训练和评估心理咨询大模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将客户心理图与多轮对话状态跟踪结合，实现动态更新心理图以反映咨询过程中的变化。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据可能无法完全捕捉真实咨询的复杂性和文化差异，需谨慎评估泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用图结构约束对话生成，确保心理一致性，可启发其他安全关键领域的合成数据生成方法。

zh.tldr: 提出Graph2Counsel框架，基于客户心理图生成合成咨询对话，解决真实数据稀缺问题，提升对话真实性和安全性。
zh.motivation: 心理健康咨询领域因隐私限制缺乏真实数据，现有合成方法忽略客户认知、情绪和行为间的结构依赖，导致心理不一致。
zh.method: 构建客户心理图编码思想、情绪和行为关系，采用结构化提示流水线（含CoT和多智能体反馈）生成咨询对话。
zh.result: 生成760个会话，专家评估在特异性、咨询师能力、真实性、对话流畅性和安全性上优于先前数据集，微调开源模型提升下游基准性能。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助研究人员快速生成高质量、心理一致的合成咨询数据，用于训练和评估心理咨询大模型。

idea_spark.idea: 将客户心理图与多轮对话状态跟踪结合，实现动态更新心理图以反映咨询过程中的变化。
idea_spark.risk: 合成数据可能无法完全捕捉真实咨询的复杂性和文化差异，需谨慎评估泛化能力。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用图结构约束对话生成，确保心理一致性，可启发其他安全关键领域的合成数据生成方法。

en.tldr: Proposes Graph2Counsel, a framework that generates synthetic counseling dialogues grounded in Client Psychological Graphs to address data scarcity while ensuring psychological consistency and safety.
en.motivation: Real counseling data is scarce due to privacy constraints; existing synthetic methods ignore structural dependencies among cognitive, emotional, and behavioral states, leading to psychologically inconsistent interactions.
en.method: Constructs Client Psychological Graphs encoding relationships among thoughts, emotions, and behaviors, and uses a structured prompting pipeline with CoT and Multi-Agent Feedback to generate dialogues.
en.result: Generates 760 sessions from 76 CPGs; expert evaluation shows superiority in specificity, counselor competence, authenticity, conversational flow, and safety; fine-tuning an open-source model improves downstream benchmarks.
en.help_to_user: Enables researchers to generate high-quality, psychologically consistent synthetic counseling data for training and evaluating mental health LLMs.

idea_spark.idea: Combine client psychological graphs with multi-turn dialogue state tracking to dynamically update the graph reflecting changes during counseling sessions.
idea_spark.risk: Synthetic data may not fully capture the complexity and cultural nuances of real counseling; careful evaluation of generalization is needed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Using graph structures to constrain dialogue generation for psychological consistency can inspire synthetic data generation methods in other safety-critical domains.

papers.title: Random Walk on Point Clouds for Feature Detection
papers.summary: The points on the point clouds that can entirely outline the shape of the model are of critical importance, as they serve as the foundation for numerous point cloud processing tasks and are widely utilized in computer graphics and computer-aided design. This study introduces a novel method, RWoDSN, for extracting such feature points, incorporating considerations of sharp-to-smooth transitions, large-to-small scales, and textural-to-detailed features. We approach feature extraction as a two-stage context-dependent analysis problem. In the first stage, we propose a novel neighborhood descriptor, termed the Disk Sampling Neighborhood (DSN), which, unlike traditional spatially and geometrically invariant approaches, preserves a matrix structure while maintaining normal neighborhood relationships. In the second stage, a random walk is performed on the DSN (RWoDSN), yielding a graph-based DSN that simultaneously accounts for the spatial distribution, topological properties, and geometric characteristics of the local surface surrounding each point. This enables the effective extraction of feature points. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed RWoDSN method achieves a recall of 0.769-22% higher than the current state-of-the-art-alongside a precision of 0.784. Furthermore, it significantly outperforms several traditional and deep-learning techniques across eight evaluation metrics.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于随机游走和圆盘采样邻域（RWoDSN）的点云特征点检测方法，在多个指标上超越现有技术。
ai.motivation: 现有方法难以同时处理尖锐到平滑过渡、多尺度以及纹理细节特征，且传统邻域描述子缺乏空间与几何信息的矩阵结构。
ai.method: 两阶段分析：第一阶段设计圆盘采样邻域（DSN）保持矩阵结构；第二阶段在DSN上执行随机游走，构建图结构以融合空间分布、拓扑和几何特征。
ai.result: 召回率0.769（比最优方法高22%），精确率0.784，在8项评估指标上优于多种传统和深度学习方法。
ai.help_to_user: 可用于点云形状分析、三维重建、计算机辅助设计等任务中的特征点提取。

idea_spark.idea: 将DSN的矩阵结构视为局部几何的“图像”，利用图卷积或注意力机制进一步学习特征。
idea_spark.risk: 随机游走参数（步长、重启概率）对结果敏感，需仔细调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受随机游走用于图节点重要性排序的启发，将其应用于点云局部邻域的特征重要性评估。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于随机游走和圆盘采样邻域（RWoDSN）的点云特征点检测方法，在多个指标上超越现有技术。
zh.motivation: 现有方法难以同时处理尖锐到平滑过渡、多尺度以及纹理细节特征，且传统邻域描述子缺乏空间与几何信息的矩阵结构。
zh.method: 两阶段分析：第一阶段设计圆盘采样邻域（DSN）保持矩阵结构；第二阶段在DSN上执行随机游走，构建图结构以融合空间分布、拓扑和几何特征。
zh.result: 召回率0.769（比最优方法高22%），精确率0.784，在8项评估指标上优于多种传统和深度学习方法。
zh.help_to_user: 可用于点云形状分析、三维重建、计算机辅助设计等任务中的特征点提取。

idea_spark.idea: 将DSN的矩阵结构视为局部几何的“图像”，利用图卷积或注意力机制进一步学习特征。
idea_spark.risk: 随机游走参数（步长、重启概率）对结果敏感，需仔细调参。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受随机游走用于图节点重要性排序的启发，将其应用于点云局部邻域的特征重要性评估。

en.tldr: Proposes a random walk on disk sampling neighborhood (RWoDSN) method for point cloud feature detection, outperforming state-of-the-art on multiple metrics.
en.motivation: Existing methods struggle with sharp-to-smooth transitions, multi-scale, and texture details; traditional descriptors lack matrix structure for spatial and geometric information.
en.method: Two-stage analysis: first, a Disk Sampling Neighborhood (DSN) preserves matrix structure; second, a random walk on DSN builds a graph that integrates spatial distribution, topology, and geometry.
en.result: Recall 0.769 (22% higher than SOTA), precision 0.784, outperforms traditional and deep learning methods on 8 evaluation metrics.
en.help_to_user: Useful for feature point extraction in point cloud shape analysis, 3D reconstruction, and CAD.

idea_spark.idea: Treat the DSN matrix as a local geometric 'image' and apply graph convolution or attention mechanisms to learn features.
idea_spark.risk: Random walk parameters (step length, restart probability) are sensitive and require careful tuning.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by random walk for node importance ranking in graphs, applied to assess feature importance in local point cloud neighborhoods.

papers.title: Online CS-based SAR Edge-Mapping
papers.summary: With modern defense applications increasingly relying on inexpensive, small Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), a major challenge lies in designing intelligent and computationally efficient onboard Automatic Target Recognition (ATR) algorithms to carry out operational objectives. This is especially critical in Synthetic Aperture Radar (SAR), where processing techniques such as ATR are often carried out post data collection, requiring onboard systems to bear the memory burden of storing the back-scattered signals. To alleviate this high cost, we propose an online, direct, edge-mapping technique which bypasses the image reconstruction step to classify scenes and targets. Furthermore, by reconstructing the scene as an edge-map we inherently promote sparsity, requiring fewer measurements and computational power than classic SAR reconstruction algorithms such as backprojection.

ai.tldr: 提出一种在线压缩感知SAR边缘映射方法，跳过图像重建直接分类，降低存储和计算开销。
ai.motivation: 小型无人机SAR系统存储和计算资源有限，传统ATR需先重建图像再分类，负担重。
ai.method: 在线直接边缘映射，利用压缩感知和稀疏性，从回波信号直接生成边缘图进行分类。
ai.result: 相比经典后向投影算法，所需测量数和计算量更少，同时保持分类能力。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解如何用压缩感知和边缘映射实现轻量级SAR目标识别。

idea_spark.idea: 将边缘映射与在线学习结合，使系统能自适应更新分类模型。
idea_spark.risk: 边缘图可能丢失纹理细节，影响复杂场景分类精度。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受压缩感知和边缘检测启发，跳过完整重建直接提取判别特征。

zh.tldr: 提出一种在线压缩感知SAR边缘映射方法，跳过图像重建直接分类，降低存储和计算开销。

idea_spark.inspiration: 受压缩感知和边缘检测启发，跳过完整重建直接提取判别特征。

en.tldr: Proposes an online CS-based SAR edge-mapping technique that bypasses image reconstruction for direct classification, reducing storage and computation.
en.motivation: Small UAV SAR systems have limited storage and compute; traditional ATR requires full image reconstruction before classification, which is costly.
en.method: Online direct edge-mapping using compressed sensing and sparsity, generating edge maps from raw backscattered signals for classification.
en.result: Requires fewer measurements and less computation than classic backprojection while maintaining classification capability.
en.help_to_user: Understand how compressed sensing and edge mapping enable lightweight SAR target recognition.

idea_spark.idea: Combine edge mapping with online learning to adapt classification models on the fly.
idea_spark.risk: Edge maps may lose texture details, potentially reducing accuracy in complex scenes.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by compressed sensing and edge detection to extract discriminative features without full reconstruction.

papers.title: Finite-Energy Weak Solutions to the Quantum Isothermal Euler System via a Logarithmic Schrödinger Approximation
papers.summary: This paper investigates the collisionless quantum hydrodynamic, or quantum Euler, system in \(\mathbb{T}^3\) with the linear pressure law \(P(ρ)=ρ\). Since this pressure is associated with the logarithmic internal energy \(f(ρ)=ρ\logρ\), the model admits a natural logarithmic Schrödinger approximation. By means of a regularized logarithmic Schrödinger equation, we rigorously construct global weak solutions to the quantum isothermal Euler system. The proof relies on the Madelung transform, the polar decomposition of the wave functions, and compactness arguments. In particular, an energy identity is used to recover the strong convergence of the hydrodynamic variables. More broadly, the analysis provides a robust Schrödinger approximation framework for QHD models whose internal energy contains an isothermal component.

ai.tldr: 本文通过正则化对数薛定谔方程，在三维环面上构造了量子等温欧拉系统的全局弱解。
ai.motivation: 量子等温欧拉系统因线性压力律与对数内能关联，缺乏全局弱解的存在性理论。
ai.method: 利用Madelung变换、波函数极分解和紧性论证，从对数薛定谔近似恢复流体变量强收敛。
ai.result: 成功构造了全局弱解，并建立了稳健的薛定谔近似框架。
ai.help_to_user: 为量子流体动力学中带等温分量的模型提供了分析工具。

idea_spark.idea: 将薛定谔方程的正则化与能量恒等式结合，以处理奇性压力。
idea_spark.risk: 三维环面紧性假设可能限制到无界区域的应用。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从量子流体到薛定谔方程的变换可启发其他非线性色散系统的弱解构造。

zh.tldr: 本文通过正则化对数薛定谔方程，在三维环面上构造了量子等温欧拉系统的全局弱解。
zh.motivation: 量子等温欧拉系统因线性压力律与对数内能关联，缺乏全局弱解的存在性理论。
zh.method: 利用Madelung变换、波函数极分解和紧性论证，从对数薛定谔近似恢复流体变量强收敛。
