idea_spark.risk: 结构化经验库的构建和维护成本较高，且过程奖励设计可能依赖领域知识。
idea_spark.inspiration: 受人类主动回忆经验的启发，将检索时机和内容选择纳入学习过程。

en.tldr: ProactAgent enables lifelong agents to proactively retrieve relevant experience from structured memory and skills, improving task success and efficiency.
en.motivation: Existing methods passively retrieve past experience only at task start or after each step, failing to identify knowledge gaps during interaction.
en.method: Builds a structured experience base (factual memory, episodic memory, behavioral skills) and uses ProactRL to model retrieval as an explicit policy action, learning when and what to retrieve via paired-branch process rewards.
en.result: Achieves 73.50% success on SciWorld and 71.28% on AlfWorld, with reduced retrieval overhead, and matches proprietary models on StuLife.
en.help_to_user: Provides a framework and algorithm for proactive retrieval in lifelong learning agents, useful for researchers and practitioners in continual learning and interactive AI.

idea_spark.idea: Model retrieval as a reinforcement learning action with process reward supervision for fine-grained control.
idea_spark.risk: Building and maintaining structured experience bases can be costly, and process reward design may require domain expertise.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspired by how humans actively recall relevant experiences when facing new situations.

papers.title: Towards Secure Logging: Characterizing and Benchmarking Logging Code Security Issues with LLMs
papers.summary: Logging code plays an important role in software systems by recording key events and behaviors, which are essential for debugging and monitoring. However, insecure logging practices can inadvertently expose sensitive information or enable attacks such as log injection, posing serious threats to system security and privacy. Prior research has examined general defects in logging code, but systematic analysis of logging code security issues remains limited, particularly in leveraging LLMs for detection and repair. In this paper, we derive a comprehensive taxonomy of logging code security issues, encompassing four common issue categories and 10 corresponding patterns. We further construct a benchmark dataset with 101 real-world logging security issue reports that have been manually reviewed and annotated. We then propose an automated framework that incorporates various contextual knowledge to evaluate LLMs' capabilities in detecting and repairing logging security issues. Our experimental results reveal a notable disparity in performance: while LLMs are moderately effective at detecting security issues (e.g., the accuracy ranges from 12.9% to 52.5% on average), they face noticeable challenges in reliably generating correct code repairs. We also find that the issue description alone improves the LLMs' detection accuracy more than the security pattern explanation or a combination of both. Overall, our findings provide actionable insights for practitioners and highlight the potential and limitations of current LLMs for secure logging.

ai.tldr: 本文系统分析了日志代码的安全问题，构建了包含4类10种模式的分类法和101个真实案例的基准数据集，并提出一个利用LLM检测和修复日志安全问题的自动化框架。实验表明LLM在检测上中等有效（准确率12.9%-52.5%），但在修复上挑战较大，且问题描述比安全模式解释更能提升检测准确率。
ai.motivation: 日志代码在软件系统中至关重要，但不安全的日志实践会泄露敏感信息或引发日志注入等攻击。现有研究多关注日志代码的一般缺陷，缺乏对日志安全问题的系统分析，尤其是利用LLM进行检测和修复的研究。
ai.method: 首先通过文献和真实案例推导出日志安全问题的分类法（4类10种模式）；然后构建包含101个经人工审核标注的真实安全报告基准数据集；最后提出一个集成多种上下文知识的自动化框架，评估LLM在检测和修复日志安全问题上的能力。
ai.result: LLM在检测日志安全问题上表现中等（平均准确率12.9%-52.5%），但在生成正确代码修复方面面临显著挑战。单独使用问题描述比安全模式解释或两者结合更能提升检测准确率。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助开发者和安全从业者了解日志代码安全问题的常见模式，以及当前LLM在检测和修复这些问题的能力与局限，从而指导更安全的日志实践和工具选择。

idea_spark.idea: 结合静态分析工具与LLM，先由静态工具定位可疑日志代码，再由LLM进行细粒度检测和修复，可能提升整体效果。
idea_spark.risk: LLM生成的修复代码可能引入新漏洞，需人工审查；基准数据集规模有限，可能影响泛化性。
idea_spark.inspiration: 探索将日志安全检测集成到CI/CD流水线中，利用LLM自动审查每次提交的日志代码变更。

en.tldr: This paper systematically analyzes security issues in logging code, derives a taxonomy of 4 categories and 10 patterns, constructs a benchmark of 101 real-world reports, and proposes an automated framework using LLMs for detection and repair. Experiments show LLMs are moderately effective in detection (accuracy 12.9%-52.5%) but struggle with repair; issue descriptions alone improve detection more than security pattern explanations.
en.motivation: Logging code is critical for debugging and monitoring, but insecure practices can leak sensitive information or enable log injection attacks. Prior work focuses on general logging defects, lacking systematic analysis of logging security issues, especially using LLMs for detection and repair.
en.method: Derive a comprehensive taxonomy of logging security issues (4 categories, 10 patterns) from literature and real cases; build a benchmark dataset of 101 manually reviewed and annotated security reports; propose an automated framework integrating various contextual knowledge to evaluate LLMs' detection and repair capabilities.
en.result: LLMs show moderate detection performance (average accuracy 12.9%-52.5%) but face significant challenges in generating correct code repairs. Issue descriptions alone improve detection accuracy more than security pattern explanations or their combination.
en.help_to_user: Helps practitioners understand common logging security issue patterns and the current capabilities and limitations of LLMs for detecting and fixing them, guiding more secure logging practices and tool selection.

idea_spark.idea: Combine static analysis tools with LLMs: static tools first flag suspicious logging code, then LLMs perform fine-grained detection and repair, potentially improving overall effectiveness.
idea_spark.risk: LLM-generated repairs may introduce new vulnerabilities and require manual review; the benchmark dataset is limited in size, affecting generalizability.
idea_spark.inspiration: Explore integrating logging security detection into CI/CD pipelines, using LLMs to automatically review logging code changes in each commit.

papers.title: MIRROR: A Hierarchical Benchmark for Metacognitive Calibration in Large Language Models
papers.summary: We introduce MIRROR, a benchmark comprising eight experiments across four metacognitive levels that evaluates whether large language models can use self-knowledge to make better decisions. We evaluate 16 models from 8 labs across approximately 250,000 evaluation instances using five independent behavioral measurement channels. Core experiments are run across the full model roster; experiments with specialized infrastructure requirements report explicitly marked model subsets. We find two phenomena with direct implications for agentic deployment: (1) compositional self-prediction fails universally -- the Compositional Calibration Error ranges from 0.500 to 0.943 on the original 15-model Exp3-v1 set (and 0.434 to 0.758 on the balanced 16-model Exp3-v2 expansion), indicating that models cannot predict their own performance on multi-domain tasks, and (2) models exhibit above-chance but imperfect domain-specific self-knowledge yet systematically fail to translate even this partial awareness into appropriate agentic action-selection -- external metacognitive control reduces the Confident Failure Rate from 0.600 to 0.143 (76% reduction at temperature 0; mean 70% at temperature 0.7 across 5 models from 4 labs). Providing models with their own calibration scores produces no significant improvement (p > 0.05); only architectural constraint is effective. This suggests that external metacognitive scaffolding -- not improved self-knowledge -- is the path to safer autonomous AI systems. Code, data, and Croissant metadata will be released publicly with the benchmark.

ai.tldr: MIRROR是一个包含八个实验、覆盖四个元认知层级的基准测试，评估大语言模型能否利用自我知识做出更好决策。
ai.motivation: 现有基准缺乏对模型元认知校准能力的系统评估，而这对安全自主部署至关重要。
ai.method: 设计八个实验，通过五个独立行为测量通道，在约25万次评估实例上测试16个模型。
ai.result: 组合自预测普遍失败（校准误差0.434-0.943）；模型虽有领域特定自我知识但无法转化为正确行动；外部元认知控制可将自信失败率降低76%，而提供校准分数无显著改善。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解当前大语言模型在自我认知与行动决策之间的差距，以及外部约束对提升安全性的重要性。

idea_spark.idea: 设计一种混合架构，将模型内部自我知识提取为显式信号，再通过外部控制器强制约束行动选择。
idea_spark.risk: 外部控制可能过度限制模型灵活性，且不同任务的最优控制策略可能不同。
idea_spark.inspiration: 人类元认知训练（如反思性判断）启发我们开发模型自我校准的迭代训练方法。

zh.motivation: 现有基准缺乏对模型元认知校准能力的系统评估，而这对安全自主部署至关重要。
zh.method: 设计八个实验，通过五个独立行为测量通道，在约25万次评估实例上测试16个模型。

en.tldr: MIRROR is a benchmark with eight experiments across four metacognitive levels, evaluating whether LLMs can use self-knowledge to make better decisions.
en.motivation: Existing benchmarks lack systematic evaluation of models' metacognitive calibration, which is critical for safe autonomous deployment.
en.method: Eight experiments are designed, testing 16 models across ~250k instances via five independent behavioral measurement channels.
en.result: Compositional self-prediction universally fails (calibration error 0.434-0.943); models have domain-specific self-knowledge but fail to translate it into correct actions; external metacognitive control reduces confident failure rate by 76%, while providing calibration scores yields no significant improvement.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand the gap between LLMs' self-knowledge and action selection, and the importance of external constraints for safety.

idea_spark.idea: Design a hybrid architecture that extracts internal self-knowledge as explicit signals and then uses an external controller to enforce action selection constraints.
idea_spark.risk: External control may overly restrict model flexibility, and optimal control strategies may vary across tasks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Human metacognitive training (e.g., reflective judgment) inspires iterative self-calibration training methods for models.

papers.title: TLSCheck 2.0: An Enhanced Memory Forensics Approach to Efficiently Detect TLS Callbacks
papers.summary: Memory analysis is a crucial technique in digital forensics that enables investigators to examine the runtime state of a system through physical memory dumps. While significant advances have been made in memory forensics, the detection and analysis of Thread Local Storage (TLS) callbacks remain challenging due to their dual nature as both legitimate Windows constructs and potential vectors for malware execution. An early version of the TlsCheck plugin received recognition in the Volatility Plugin Contest 2024. In this paper, we present an enhanced version of TlsCheck for Volatility 3, designed to detect and analyze TLS callbacks in process memory. It implements precise detection of TLS callback tables through analysis of PE headers and memory structures, combined with disassembly of identified callback routines. The plugin supports both 32-bit and 64-bit architectures, offering investigators insights into callback locations, assembly behavior, and potential signs of suspicious activity. To enhance detection, we incorporate pattern matching using custom regular expressions and YARA rules, helping analysts identify specific code patterns or suspicious constructs within TLS callbacks. The framework also includes instruction-level analysis to highlight behavior often linked to malware, such as anti-debugging, code injection, and process manipulation. This implementation significantly improves defenders' ability to detect and investigate TLS-based threats during memory forensics, supporting more effective malware analysis and incident response operations.

ai.tldr: 提出增强版TlsCheck 2.0插件，用于Volatility 3，通过PE头分析、YARA规则和指令级分析高效检测内存中的TLS回调，支持32/64位架构。
ai.motivation: TLS回调在内存取证中难以检测，因其既是合法Windows机制又可能被恶意软件利用。
ai.method: 分析PE头和内存结构定位TLS回调表，反汇编回调例程，结合正则表达式和YARA规则进行模式匹配，并实施指令级行为分析。
ai.result: 显著提升检测TLS相关威胁的能力，支持反调试、代码注入等恶意行为识别。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助取证分析师在内存转储中快速发现隐藏的TLS回调，提高恶意软件分析和应急响应效率。

idea_spark.idea: 利用机器学习模型对TLS回调的指令序列进行分类，自动区分合法回调与恶意回调。
idea_spark.risk: 恶意软件可能通过混淆或动态生成TLS回调表来规避静态检测。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴Volatility插件竞赛的社区驱动模式，开发针对其他隐蔽执行路径（如APC、DPC）的专用检测插件。

zh.tldr: 提出增强版TlsCheck 2.0插件，用于Volatility 3，通过PE头分析、YARA规则和指令级分析高效检测内存中的TLS回调，支持32/64位架构。
zh.motivation: TLS回调在内存取证中难以检测，因其既是合法Windows机制又可能被恶意软件利用。
zh.method: 分析PE头和内存结构定位TLS回调表，反汇编回调例程，结合正则表达式和YARA规则进行模式匹配，并实施指令级行为分析。
zh.result: 显著提升检测TLS相关威胁的能力，支持反调试、代码注入等恶意行为识别。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助取证分析师在内存转储中快速发现隐藏的TLS回调，提高恶意软件分析和应急响应效率。

idea_spark.idea: 利用机器学习模型对TLS回调的指令序列进行分类，自动区分合法回调与恶意回调。
idea_spark.risk: 恶意软件可能通过混淆或动态生成TLS回调表来规避静态检测。
idea_spark.inspiration: 借鉴Volatility插件竞赛的社区驱动模式，开发针对其他隐蔽执行路径（如APC、DPC）的专用检测插件。

en.tldr: Proposes an enhanced TlsCheck 2.0 plugin for Volatility 3 that efficiently detects TLS callbacks in memory via PE header analysis, YARA rules, and instruction-level analysis, supporting both 32-bit and 64-bit architectures.
en.motivation: TLS callbacks are challenging to detect in memory forensics due to their dual nature as legitimate Windows constructs and potential malware vectors.
en.method: Analyzes PE headers and memory structures to locate TLS callback tables, disassembles callback routines, applies custom regex and YARA rules for pattern matching, and performs instruction-level behavior analysis.
en.result: Significantly improves detection of TLS-based threats, enabling identification of anti-debugging, code injection, and process manipulation behaviors.
en.help_to_user: Helps forensic analysts quickly uncover hidden TLS callbacks in memory dumps, enhancing malware analysis and incident response.

idea_spark.idea: Use machine learning to classify instruction sequences of TLS callbacks, distinguishing legitimate from malicious ones automatically.
idea_spark.risk: Malware may evade static detection by obfuscating or dynamically generating TLS callback tables.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage the community-driven plugin contest model to develop specialized detection plugins for other stealthy execution paths like APC or DPC.

papers.title: Can LLMs Infer Conversational Agent Users' Personality Traits from Chat History?
papers.summary: Sensitive information, such as knowledge about an individual's personality, can be can be misused to influence behavior (e.g., via personalized messaging). To assess to what extent an individual's personality can be inferred from user interactions with LLM-based conversational agents (CAs), we analyze and quantify related privacy risks of using CAs. We collected actual ChatGPT logs from N=668 participants, containing 62,090 individual chats, and report statistics about the different types of shared data and use cases. We fine-tuned RoBERTa-base text classification models to infer personality traits from CA interactions. The findings show that these models achieve trait inference with accuracy (ternary classification) better than random in multiple cases. For example, for extraversion, accuracy improves by +44% relative to the baseline on interactions for relationships and personal reflection. This research highlights how interactions with CAs pose privacy risks and provides fine-grained insights into the level of risk associated with different types of interactions.

ai.tldr: 研究通过分析ChatGPT用户聊天记录，发现微调后的RoBERTa模型能以高于随机的准确率推断用户人格特质，揭示了对话代理的隐私风险。
ai.motivation: 评估从LLM对话代理交互中推断用户人格特质的可能性，量化相关隐私风险。
ai.method: 收集668名参与者的62090条ChatGPT日志，微调RoBERTa-base模型进行人格特质三元分类。
ai.result: 模型在多个场景下推断准确率优于随机，例如外向性推断在关系与个人反思类交互中相对基线提升44%。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解与对话代理交互时人格信息被推断的风险，增强隐私保护意识。

idea_spark.idea: 设计隐私保护机制，如差分隐私或对抗性扰动，防止模型从对话中准确推断人格。
idea_spark.risk: 模型可能被恶意利用进行个性化操控或歧视性决策。
idea_spark.inspiration: 启发开发透明化隐私风险提示系统，在交互前告知用户潜在推断能力。

idea_spark.inspiration: 启发开发透明化隐私风险提示系统，在交互前告知用户潜在推断能力。

en.tldr: This study shows that fine-tuned RoBERTa models can infer users' personality traits from ChatGPT chat logs with above-random accuracy, highlighting privacy risks of conversational agents.
en.motivation: To assess the extent to which personality traits can be inferred from LLM-based conversational agent interactions and quantify associated privacy risks.
en.method: Collected 62,090 ChatGPT logs from 668 participants and fine-tuned RoBERTa-base models for ternary personality trait classification.
en.result: Models achieved better-than-random accuracy in multiple cases; e.g., extraversion inference improved by +44% relative to baseline on relationship and personal reflection interactions.
en.help_to_user: Raises awareness about privacy risks of personality inference from conversational agent interactions.

idea_spark.idea: Develop privacy-preserving techniques such as differential privacy or adversarial perturbations to prevent accurate personality inference.
idea_spark.risk: Malicious use could enable personalized manipulation or discriminatory decisions based on inferred traits.
idea_spark.inspiration: Inspires transparent privacy risk indicators that inform users about potential inference capabilities before interaction.

papers.title: Insights into Security-Related AI-Generated Pull Requests
papers.summary: Recent years have experienced growing contributions of AI coding agents that assist human developers in various software engineering tasks. However, this growing AI-assisted autonomy raises questions about security and trust. In this paper, we analyze more than 33,000 AI-generated pull requests (PRs) and identify 675 security-related submissions made by agentic AIs. Then we examine the security-related PRs with a focus on recurring security weaknesses, review outcomes and latency, commit message quality, and rejection reasons. The results show that security-related AI PRs introduce a small set of recurring weaknesses such as regex inefficiencies, injection flaws, and path traversal. Many flawed contributions are still merged, while rejections often arise from social or process factors such as inactivity or missing test coverage. The commit message quality of AI PRs has a limited effect on acceptance or latency, in contrast to human PRs reported in previous studies. We also extend existing rejection taxonomies by adding categories that are unique to AI-generated security contributions. These findings offer new insights into the strengths and shortcomings of autonomous coding systems in secure software development.

ai.tldr: 分析了超过33,000个AI生成的拉取请求，识别出675个安全相关的提交，发现常见的安全弱点（如正则表达式低效、注入缺陷、路径遍历），并探讨了审查结果、延迟、提交信息质量和拒绝原因。
ai.motivation: 随着AI编码代理在软件开发中的广泛应用，其安全性和可信度成为关键问题，需要系统性地评估AI生成拉取请求中的安全风险。
ai.method: 收集并分析33,000多个AI生成的拉取请求，筛选出675个安全相关提交，通过分类和统计方法研究安全弱点、审查结果、提交信息质量及拒绝原因。
ai.result: 安全相关的AI拉取请求存在少量重复出现的弱点（如正则表达式低效、注入缺陷、路径遍历）；许多有缺陷的贡献仍被合并，拒绝常因社交或流程因素（如不活跃、缺少测试覆盖）；提交信息质量对接受率或延迟影响有限；扩展了现有拒绝分类法，增加了AI特有类别。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助开发者和研究者了解AI生成代码的安全风险模式，以及审查流程中的关键因素，从而改进AI辅助开发的安全实践。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个自动化工具，在AI生成拉取请求时实时检测常见安全弱点，并提示开发者补充测试覆盖。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖自动化检测可能忽略上下文相关的安全漏洞，且工具本身可能被绕过。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合静态分析和社交因素（如活跃度）来预测AI拉取请求的合并风险，可能提升安全审查效率。

zh.tldr: 分析了超过33,000个AI生成的拉取请求，识别出675个安全相关的提交，发现常见的安全弱点（如正则表达式低效、注入缺陷、路径遍历），并探讨了审查结果、延迟、提交信息质量和拒绝原因。
zh.motivation: 随着AI编码代理在软件开发中的广泛应用，其安全性和可信度成为关键问题，需要系统性地评估AI生成拉取请求中的安全风险。
zh.method: 收集并分析33,000多个AI生成的拉取请求，筛选出675个安全相关提交，通过分类和统计方法研究安全弱点、审查结果、提交信息质量及拒绝原因。
zh.result: 安全相关的AI拉取请求存在少量重复出现的弱点（如正则表达式低效、注入缺陷、路径遍历）；许多有缺陷的贡献仍被合并，拒绝常因社交或流程因素（如不活跃、缺少测试覆盖）；提交信息质量对接受率或延迟影响有限；扩展了现有拒绝分类法，增加了AI特有类别。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助开发者和研究者了解AI生成代码的安全风险模式，以及审查流程中的关键因素，从而改进AI辅助开发的安全实践。

idea_spark.idea: 开发一个自动化工具，在AI生成拉取请求时实时检测常见安全弱点，并提示开发者补充测试覆盖。
idea_spark.risk: 过度依赖自动化检测可能忽略上下文相关的安全漏洞，且工具本身可能被绕过。
idea_spark.inspiration: 结合静态分析和社交因素（如活跃度）来预测AI拉取请求的合并风险，可能提升安全审查效率。

en.tldr: Analyzed over 33,000 AI-generated pull requests, identified 675 security-related submissions, found recurring weaknesses (regex inefficiencies, injection flaws, path traversal), and examined review outcomes, latency, commit message quality, and rejection reasons.
en.motivation: With the growing use of AI coding agents in software development, their security and trustworthiness become critical, necessitating a systematic evaluation of security risks in AI-generated pull requests.
en.method: Collected and analyzed over 33,000 AI-generated pull requests, filtered 675 security-related submissions, and studied security weaknesses, review outcomes, commit message quality, and rejection reasons through classification and statistical analysis.
en.result: Security-related AI pull requests exhibit a small set of recurring weaknesses (e.g., regex inefficiencies, injection flaws, path traversal); many flawed contributions are still merged, rejections often due to social or process factors (e.g., inactivity, missing test coverage); commit message quality has limited effect on acceptance or latency; extended existing rejection taxonomy with AI-specific categories.
en.help_to_user: Helps developers and researchers understand security risk patterns in AI-generated code and key factors in the review process, thereby improving secure practices in AI-assisted development.

idea_spark.idea: Develop an automated tool that detects common security weaknesses in real-time when AI generates pull requests and prompts developers to add test coverage.
idea_spark.risk: Over-reliance on automated detection may miss context-dependent vulnerabilities, and the tool itself could be bypassed.
idea_spark.inspiration: Combining static analysis with social factors (e.g., activity level) to predict merge risk of AI pull requests could improve security review efficiency.

papers.title: Assessing the Challenges of Collective Perception via V2I Communications in High-Speed Scenarios with Open Road Testing
papers.summary: This paper presents a comprehensive end-to-end evaluation of an infrastructure-assisted collective perception (ICP) system deployed on a highway using ITS-G5 technology. Open-road tests were conducted in the Bizkaia Connected Corridor (BCC), an operational corridor which covers a winding highway, enabling a realistic assessment of system performance in diverse traffic scenarios. The evaluation included three main aspects: (1) end-to-end Vehicle-to-Everything (V2X) communication latency, with a breakdown of delays introduced by each system component; (2) the effective range of ITS-G5 communications between vehicles and infrastructure; and (3) the perception system, using an independent sensor setup for ground truth annotation to account for errors beyond the detection model, such as synchronization, localization, and calibration inaccuracies. The results reveal that object detection and asynchronous transmission of collective perception messages (CPMs) are major latency bottlenecks, with results showing that synchronizing CPM transmission with local perception can reduce delays by up to 33%. Additionally, onboard perception struggles with detecting objects beyond 50 meters, highlighting the importance of collective perception in highway environments, where communication ranges significantly exceed detection limits. The findings provide valuable insights to optimize ICP deployments, supporting safer and more efficient cooperative mobility systems.

ai.tldr: 本文在真实高速公路上评估了基于ITS-G5的基础设施辅助集体感知系统，分析了端到端延迟、通信范围和感知性能。
ai.motivation: 现有集体感知研究多基于仿真，缺乏真实高速公路场景下的端到端评估，尤其是高动态环境下的延迟和感知瓶颈。
ai.method: 在Bizkaia连接走廊进行开放道路测试，使用独立传感器进行地面真值标注，分解V2X通信延迟，并对比同步与非同步CPM传输。
ai.result: 目标检测和异步CPM传输是主要延迟瓶颈，同步传输可减少33%延迟；车载感知在50米外失效，而通信范围远超此距离。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解真实高速公路环境下集体感知系统的性能瓶颈和优化方向。

idea_spark.idea: 设计自适应CPM发送频率，根据车辆速度和感知范围动态调整，以平衡延迟和信道负载。
idea_spark.risk: 真实环境测试成本高，且ITS-G5在密集车流下的信道拥塞可能影响同步效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用路边单元作为计算节点，对感知数据进行预处理和融合，减少车载端延迟。

zh.tldr: 本文在真实高速公路上评估了基于ITS-G5的基础设施辅助集体感知系统，分析了端到端延迟、通信范围和感知性能。

idea_spark.risk: 真实环境测试成本高，且ITS-G5在密集车流下的信道拥塞可能影响同步效果。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用路边单元作为计算节点，对感知数据进行预处理和融合，减少车载端延迟。

en.tldr: This paper evaluates an infrastructure-assisted collective perception system on a real highway using ITS-G5, analyzing end-to-end latency, communication range, and perception performance.
en.motivation: Existing studies on collective perception rely on simulations, lacking end-to-end evaluation in real highway scenarios, especially latency and perception bottlenecks under high dynamics.
en.method: Open-road tests were conducted in the Bizkaia Connected Corridor, using independent sensors for ground truth annotation, decomposing V2X communication latency, and comparing synchronous vs. asynchronous CPM transmission.
en.result: Object detection and asynchronous CPM transmission are major latency bottlenecks; synchronization reduces delays by 33%. Onboard perception fails beyond 50 m, while communication range far exceeds detection limits.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand performance bottlenecks and optimization directions of collective perception systems in real highway environments.

idea_spark.idea: Design adaptive CPM transmission frequency that adjusts based on vehicle speed and perception range to balance latency and channel load.
idea_spark.risk: Real-world testing is costly, and ITS-G5 channel congestion in dense traffic may affect synchronization effectiveness.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverage roadside units as computing nodes to preprocess and fuse perception data, reducing onboard latency.

papers.title: Text Steganography with Dynamic Codebook and Multimodal Large Language Model
papers.summary: With the popularity of the large language models (LLMs), text steganography has achieved remarkable performance. However, existing methods still have some issues: (1) For the white-box paradigm, this steganography behavior is prone to exposure due to sharing the off-the-shelf language model between Alice and Bob.(2) For the black-box paradigm, these methods lack flexibility and practicality since Alice and Bob should share the fixed codebook while sharing a specific extracting prompt for each steganographic sentence. In order to improve the security and practicality, we introduce a black-box text steganography with a dynamic codebook and multimodal large language model. Specifically, we first construct a dynamic codebook via some shared session configuration and a multimodal large language model. Then an encrypted steganographic mapping is designed to embed secret messages during the steganographic caption generation. Furthermore, we introduce a feedback optimization mechanism based on reject sampling to ensure accurate extraction of secret messages. Experimental results show that the proposed method outperforms existing white-box text steganography methods in terms of embedding capacity and text quality. Meanwhile, the proposed method has achieved better practicality and flexibility than the existing black-box paradigm in some popular online social networks.

ai.tldr: 提出一种基于动态码本和多模态大语言模型的黑盒文本隐写方法，通过共享会话配置构建动态码本，设计加密隐写映射嵌入秘密消息，并引入拒绝采样反馈优化机制，在嵌入容量和文本质量上优于现有白盒方法，在在线社交网络中具有更好的实用性和灵活性。
ai.motivation: 现有白盒文本隐写因共享现成语言模型易暴露，黑盒方法因固定码本和特定提取提示缺乏灵活性和实用性。
ai.method: 利用共享会话配置和多模态大语言模型构建动态码本，设计加密隐写映射生成隐写标题，并采用基于拒绝采样的反馈优化机制确保秘密消息准确提取。
ai.result: 在嵌入容量和文本质量上优于现有白盒方法，在在线社交网络中比现有黑盒范式具有更好的实用性和灵活性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解一种更安全、实用的文本隐写技术，可用于隐蔽通信或数据隐藏场景。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多模态大语言模型和动态码本，实现无需固定码本的黑盒隐写。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖多模态大语言模型的可用性和共享会话配置的安全性，可能面临模型攻击或配置泄露风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用大语言模型的生成能力和多模态信息构建动态映射，启发更灵活的信息隐藏方法。

zh.tldr: 提出一种基于动态码本和多模态大语言模型的黑盒文本隐写方法，通过共享会话配置构建动态码本，设计加密隐写映射嵌入秘密消息，并引入拒绝采样反馈优化机制，在嵌入容量和文本质量上优于现有白盒方法，在在线社交网络中具有更好的实用性和灵活性。

zh.method: 利用共享会话配置和多模态大语言模型构建动态码本，设计加密隐写映射生成隐写标题，并采用基于拒绝采样的反馈优化机制确保秘密消息准确提取。
zh.result: 在嵌入容量和文本质量上优于现有白盒方法，在在线社交网络中比现有黑盒范式具有更好的实用性和灵活性。
zh.help_to_user: 帮助用户了解一种更安全、实用的文本隐写技术，可用于隐蔽通信或数据隐藏场景。

idea_spark.idea: 结合多模态大语言模型和动态码本，实现无需固定码本的黑盒隐写。
idea_spark.risk: 依赖多模态大语言模型的可用性和共享会话配置的安全性，可能面临模型攻击或配置泄露风险。
idea_spark.inspiration: 利用大语言模型的生成能力和多模态信息构建动态映射，启发更灵活的信息隐藏方法。

en.tldr: Proposes a black-box text steganography method using a dynamic codebook and multimodal large language model, constructing the codebook via shared session configuration, embedding secret messages through encrypted steganographic mapping, and employing reject-sampling-based feedback optimization, outperforming white-box methods in embedding capacity and text quality with better practicality and flexibility on online social networks.
en.motivation: Existing white-box text steganography risks exposure due to shared language models, while black-box methods lack flexibility and practicality due to fixed codebooks and specific extraction prompts.
en.method: Constructs a dynamic codebook using shared session configuration and a multimodal large language model, designs encrypted steganographic mapping for caption generation, and introduces reject-sampling-based feedback optimization for accurate extraction.
en.result: Outperforms existing white-box methods in embedding capacity and text quality, and achieves better practicality and flexibility than black-box paradigms on popular online social networks.
en.help_to_user: Helps users understand a more secure and practical text steganography technique for covert communication or data hiding.

idea_spark.idea: Combines multimodal large language models with dynamic codebooks to enable black-box steganography without fixed codebooks.
idea_spark.risk: Relies on availability of multimodal large language models and security of shared session configuration, risking model attacks or configuration leaks.
idea_spark.inspiration: Leverages generative capabilities of large language models and multimodal information to build dynamic mappings, inspiring more flexible information hiding methods.

papers.title: Decoupling Speculation from Merit: The Identity-Bound Asset Integrity Model (IBAIM) for Sustainable Web3 Gaming
papers.summary: The rapid collapse of decentralized game economies, often characterized by the \textit{death spiral,} remains the most formidable barrier to the mass adoption of Web3 gaming. This paper proposes that the sustainability of an open game economy is predicated on three necessary and sufficient conditions: Anti-Sybil Resilience, Anti-Capital Dominance, and Anti-Inflationary Saturation. The first section establishes a theoretical proof of these conditions, arguing that the absence of any single dimension leads to systemic failure. The second section explores the dialectical relationship between these dimensions, illustrating how unchecked automation and capital-driven monopolies accelerate asset hyperinflation. In the third section, we introduce the Identity-Bound Asset Integrity Model (IBAIM) as a comprehensive technical solution. IBAIM utilizes Zero-Knowledge (ZK) biometric hashing and Account Abstraction (AA) to anchor asset utility to unique human identities through a privacy-preserving and regulatory-compliant architecture. By exogenizing biometric verification to trusted local environments and utilizing Zero-Knowledge Proofs of Identity (zk-PoI), the model ensures absolute user privacy. Furthermore, by implementing an Asymmetric Utility Decay (AUD) engine-whereby assets suffer a vertical 50% utility cliff upon secondary transfer-and an entropy-driven thermodynamic degradation mechanism., the model successfully decouples financial speculation from in-game merit. Finally, we apply this framework to analyze prominent historical failures in the GameFi sector, demonstrating that their collapse was an inevitable consequence of violating these core economic constraints. Our findings suggest that trading a degree of asset liquidity for system integrity is the only viable path toward long-term economic viability in decentralized virtual worlds.

ai.tldr: 提出身份绑定资产完整性模型（IBAIM），通过零知识生物识别哈希和账户抽象将资产效用锚定到唯一人类身份，并引入非对称效用衰减和热力学退化机制，以解决Web3游戏经济中的死亡螺旋问题。
ai.motivation: 去中心化游戏经济频繁崩溃（死亡螺旋）是Web3游戏大规模采用的主要障碍，现有方案未能同时满足反女巫、反资本主导和反通胀饱和三个必要条件。
ai.method: 理论证明三个必要条件；设计IBAIM模型，包括零知识生物识别哈希、账户抽象、零知识身份证明、非对称效用衰减（二次转移后资产效用垂直下降50%）和熵驱动热力学退化机制。
ai.result: 通过分析GameFi历史失败案例，证明其崩溃源于违反核心经济约束；模型通过牺牲部分资产流动性换取系统完整性，实现长期经济可持续性。
ai.help_to_user: 帮助理解Web3游戏经济可持续性的关键条件，以及如何通过隐私保护的身份绑定和资产效用衰减机制设计更稳健的游戏经济系统。

idea_spark.idea: 将热力学熵增概念引入数字资产设计，使资产价值随交易次数自然衰减，模拟物理世界的资源消耗。
idea_spark.risk: 用户可能抵制资产流动性受限；生物识别哈希的本地执行依赖用户设备安全，存在侧信道攻击风险；监管合规性需进一步验证。
idea_spark.inspiration: 从热力学第二定律和生物识别隐私保护技术中汲取灵感，将物理世界的不可逆性和唯一性映射到数字资产经济中。

en.tldr: Proposes the Identity-Bound Asset Integrity Model (IBAIM) that anchors asset utility to unique human identities via zero-knowledge biometric hashing and account abstraction, with asymmetric utility decay and thermodynamic degradation, to solve the death spiral in Web3 gaming economies.
en.motivation: Decentralized game economies frequently collapse (death spiral), hindering Web3 gaming mass adoption; existing solutions fail to simultaneously satisfy anti-Sybil, anti-capital dominance, and anti-inflationary saturation conditions.
en.method: Theoretically proves three necessary conditions; designs IBAIM including zero-knowledge biometric hashing, account abstraction, zero-knowledge proofs of identity, asymmetric utility decay (50% utility cliff on secondary transfer), and entropy-driven thermodynamic degradation.
en.result: Analysis of historical GameFi failures shows collapse was inevitable due to violating core economic constraints; the model trades some asset liquidity for system integrity to achieve long-term economic sustainability.
en.help_to_user: Helps understand key conditions for sustainable Web3 game economies and how privacy-preserving identity binding and asset utility decay can design more robust game economic systems.

idea_spark.idea: Introduce thermodynamic entropy into digital asset design, causing asset value to naturally decay with each transaction, simulating physical resource consumption.
idea_spark.risk: Users may resist reduced asset liquidity; local execution of biometric hashing depends on device security, risking side-channel attacks; regulatory compliance needs further validation.
idea_spark.inspiration: Draws inspiration from the second law of thermodynamics and biometric privacy technologies, mapping physical irreversibility and uniqueness to digital asset economies.
